title: Heat stroke
definition: Elevation of core body temperature above 40.6 degrees centigrade due to environmental heat exposure and a failure of thermoregulation. This is a potentially fatal disorder, particularly in infants and children.
exclusions: Exertional heat stroke

title: Heat syncope
definition: Fainting attributable to exposure to heat

title: Heat exhaustion due to fluid depletion
definition: A failure of thermoregulatory sweating on exposure to heat as a result of water deprivation and/or inadequate replacement of fluids lost through sweating or by other means (e.g. severe diarrhoea). If untreated this may progress to heat stroke.
inclusions: Heat prostration due to water depletion

title: Hypothermia
exclusions: Hypothermia, not associated with low environmental temperature; Frostbite

title: Chilblains
definition: Chilblains are the result of cold-induced damage principally to the microvasculature of acral skin in susceptible individuals. They are commonest in the winter months in cold, damp climates. They present as itchy or painful red-purple macules, papules or plaques, most commonly affecting the fingers and toes, though other body extremities including the heels, nose and ears can be involved. Because of insulation from core body temperature, other areas, especially the lateral thighs, may be involved in areas where there is a thick layer of subcutaneous fat.

title: Immersion hand or foot
definition: Injury to the skin and soft tissues of the feet and, less commonly, the hands due to prolonged exposure to non-freezing cold and wet conditions. Originally reported in soldiers as trench foot during World War I, it is now more commonly seen in the homeless either because of vagrancy or as the result of conflict. Affected extremities are initially cold, numb, swollen and pulseless~ this is followed by a period of intense hyperaemia and pain which then gives way to increased sweating and cold sensitivity.

Physical maltreatment
definition: Non-accidental acts of physical force that result, or have reasonable potential to result, in physical harm or that evoke significant fear. The category is applied to the victim of the maltreatment, not the perpetrator.

title: Sexual maltreatment
definition: In adults, forced or coerced sexual acts or sexual acts with someone who is unable to consent~ in children, sexual acts involving a child that are intended to provide sexual gratification to an adult. The category is applied to the victim of the maltreatment, not the perpetrator.

title: Psychological maltreatment
definition: Non-accidental verbal or symbolic acts that result in significant psychological harm. The category is applied to the victim of the maltreatment, not the perpetrator.

Cut, puncture or tear, as mode of injury or harm
definition: The cut or puncture occurs when a solid organ or blood vessel or nerve is unintentionally lacerated or otherwise damaged during a surgical or medical procedure. The cut or puncture must not be required for the successful completion of the procedure. A perforation occurs when a hollow viscous, such as the bowel or urinary bladder, is injured during a surgical procedure such that the contents of the viscous leak into the surrounding tissues or space.

title: Burn arising during procedure, as mode of injury or harm
definition: A burn occurs when tissue is damaged by heat, electricity or fire. It can occur, for example, as the direct result of cautery equipment, warming efforts, or because of a fire.
exclusions: Burn from ionising radiation

title: Air embolism, as mode of injury
Other specified embolisation, as mode of injury or harm
Embolisation, as mode of injury or harm, unspecified
Foreign body accidentally left in body, as mode of injury or harm
definition: A foreign body is any solid material not normally found in the human body. It is accidentally left in the body if there was no specific intention to keep it in the body, either because it was indicated for medical purposes or because it was unsafe to retrieve.
exclusions: Foreign body accidentally left in body without injury or harm

title: Failure of sterile precautions, as mode of injury or harm

exclusions: Failure of sterile precautions without injury or harm

Hazardous alcohol use
definition: A pattern of alcohol use that appreciably increases the risk of harmful physical or mental health consequences to the user or to others to an extent that warrants attention and advice from health professionals. The increased risk may be from the frequency of alcohol use, from the amount used on a given occasion, or from risky behaviours associated with alcohol use or the context of use, or from a combination of these. The risk may be related to short-term effects of alcohol or to longer-term cumulative effects on physical or mental health or functioning. Hazardous alcohol use has not yet reached the level of having caused harm to physical or mental health of the user or others around the user. The pattern of alcohol use often persists in spite of awareness of increased risk of harm to the user or to others.
exclusions: Disorders due to use of alcohol

title: Hazardous nicotine use
definition: A pattern of nicotine use that appreciably increases the risk of harmful physical or mental health consequences to the user or to others to an extent that warrants attention and advice from health professionals. Most often nicotine is consumed in the form of tobacco, but there are also other forms of nicotine delivery (e.g., nicotine vapour). Hazardous nicotine use has not yet reached the level of having caused harm to physical or mental health of the user or others around the user. The pattern of nicotine use often persists in spite of awareness of increased risk of harm to the user or to others. This category is not intended to include the use of nicotine replacement therapies under medical supervision when these are used as part of attempts to stop or reduce smoking.
exclusions: Tobacco use; Disorders due to use of nicotine

title: Hazardous gambling or betting
definition: Hazardous gambling and betting refers to a pattern of gambling or betting that appreciably increases the risk of harmful physical or mental health consequences to the individual or to others around the individual. The increased risk may be from the frequency of gambling or betting, the amount of time spent on these activities, the context of gambling or betting, the neglect of other activities and priorities, risky behaviours associated with gambling or betting or its context, the adverse consequences of gambling or betting, or from the combination of these factors. The pattern of gambling or betting often persists in spite of awareness of increased risk of harm to the individual or to others. This category may be used when the pattern of gambling or betting warrants attention and advice from health professionals but does not meet the diagnostic requirements for Gambling Disorder.
exclusions: Gambling disorder

title: Personality difficulty
definition: Personality difficulty refers to pronounced personality characteristics that may affect treatment or health services but do not rise to the level of severity to merit a diagnosis of Personality disorder. Personality difficulty is characterised by long-standing difficulties (e.g., at least 2 years), in the individual’s way of experiencing and thinking about the self, others and the world. In contrast to Personality disorders, these difficulties are manifested in cognitive and emotional experience and expression only intermittently (e.g., during times of stress) or at low intensity. The difficulties are associated with some problems in functioning but these are insufficiently severe to cause notable disruption in social, occupational, and interpersonal relationships and may be limited to specific relationships or situations.

Acute stress reaction
definition: Acute stress reaction refers to the development of transient emotional, somatic, cognitive, or behavioural symptoms as a result of exposure to an event or situation (either short- or long-lasting) of an extremely threatening or horrific nature (e.g., natural or human-made disasters, combat, serious accidents, sexual violence, assault). Symptoms may include autonomic signs of anxiety (e.g., tachycardia, sweating, flushing), being in a daze, confusion, sadness, anxiety, anger, despair, overactivity, inactivity, social withdrawal, or stupor. The response to the stressor is considered to be normal given the severity of the stressor, and usually begins to subside within a few days after the event or following removal from the threatening situation.

title: Post COVID-19 condition
definition: Post COVID-19 condition occurs in individuals with a history of probable or confirmed SARS CoV-2 infection, usually 3 months from the onset of COVID-19 with symptoms, and that last for at least 2 months and cannot be explained by an alternative diagnosis. Common symptoms include fatigue, shortness of breath, cognitive dysfunction but also others, and generally have an impact on everyday functioning. Symptoms may be new onset following initial recovery from an acute COVID-19 episode or persist from the initial illness. Symptoms may also fluctuate or relapse over time.

Attention functions [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in concentrating on doing something for ten minutes?

title: Memory functions [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in remembering to do important things?

title: Solving problems [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in analysing and finding solutions to problems in day to day life?

title: Basic learning [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in learning a new task, for example, learning how to get a new place?

title: Communicating with - receiving - spoken messages [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in generally understanding what people say?

title: Conversation [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in starting and maintaining a conversation?

title: Maintaining a standing position [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in standing for long periods such as 30 minutes?

title: Changing body position - standing [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in standing up from sitting down?

title: Moving around within the home [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in moving around inside your home?

title: Moving around outside the home and other buildings [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in getting out of your home?

title: Walking [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in walking a long distance such as a kilometre (or equivalent)?

title: Washing oneself [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in washing your whole body?

title: Dressing [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in getting dressed?

title: Eating [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in eating?

title: Carrying out daily routine [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in staying by yourself for a few days?

title: Relating with strangers [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in dealing with people you do not know?

title: Informal relationship with friends - maintaining [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in maintaining a friendship?

title: Family relationships [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in getting along with people who are close to you?

title: Informal relationship with friends - making new friends [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in making new friends?

title: Intimate relationships [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in sexual activities?

title: Taking care of household responsibilities [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in taking care of your household responsibilities?

title: Doing most important household tasks [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in doing most important household tasks well?

title: Getting all needed housework done [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in getting all the household work done that you need to do?

title: Getting household work done quickly [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in getting your household work done as quickly as needed?

title: Difficulties in daily work or school [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in your day to day work/school?

title: Doing most important work or school task [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in doing your most important work/school tasks well?

title: Getting all needed work or school work done [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in getting all the work done that you need to do?

title: Getting remunerative work or school work done quickly [WHODAS]
definition: Because of your health condition, in the past 30 days, how much difficulty did you have in getting your work done as quickly as needed?

title: Recreation and leisure [WHODAS]
definition: In the past 30 days, how much of a problem did you have in joining in community activities (for example: festivities, religious or other activities) in the same way as anyone else can?

title: Problems by barriers [WHODAS]
definition: In the past 30 days, how much of a problem did you have because of barriers or hindrances in the world around you?

title: Human rights [WHODAS]
definition: In the past 30 days, how much of a problem did you have living with dignity because of the attitudes and actions of others?

title: Time spent on health condition [WHODAS]
definition: In the past 30 days, how much time did you spend on your health condition, or its consequences?

title: Emotional effect of health condition [WHODAS]
definition: In the past 30 days, how much have you been emotionally affected by your health condition?

title: Health drain on financial resources [WHODAS]
definition: In the past 30 days, how much has your health been a drain on the financial resources of you or your family?

title: Health problems causing family problems [WHODAS]
definition: In the past 30 days, how much of a problem did your family have because of your health problems?

title: Problems in relaxation or pleasure [WHODAS]
definition: In the past 30 days, how much of a problem did you have in doing things by yourself for relaxation or pleasure?

title: Seeing and related functions [BMDS]
definition: How much difficulty do you have seeing things at a distance [without glasses]?

title: Hearing and vestibular functions [BMDS]
definition: How much difficulty do you have hearing [without hearing aids]?

title: Energy and drive functions
definition: General mental functions of physiological and psychological mechanisms that cause the individual to move towards satisfying specific needs and general goals in a persistent manner.
exclusions: Consciousness functions; Emotional functions; Psychomotor functions; Sleep functions; Temperament and personality functions

title: Sleep functions
definition: General mental functions of periodic, reversible and selective physical and mental disengagement from one's immediate environment accompanied by characteristic physiological changes.
exclusions: Attention functions; Consciousness functions; Energy and drive functions; Psychomotor functions

title: Attention functions
definition: Specific mental functions of focusing on an external stimulus or internal experience for the required period of time.
exclusions: Consciousness functions; Energy and drive functions; Memory functions; Perceptual functions; Psychomotor functions; Sleep functions

title: Memory functions
definition: Specific mental functions of registering and storing information and retrieving it as needed.
inclusions: immediate memory; recent memory; remote memory; memory span; remembering
exclusions: Attention functions; Calculation functions; Consciousness functions; Higher-level cognitive functions; intellectual functioning; Mental functions of language; Orientation functions; Perceptual functions; Thought functions

title: Emotional functions
definition: Specific mental functions related to the feeling and affective components of the processes of the mind.
inclusions: affect; sadness; tension; lability of emotion; flattening of affect
exclusions: Energy and drive functions; Temperament and personality functions

title: Sensation of pain
definition: Sensation of unpleasant feeling indicating potential or actual damage to some body structure.
inclusions: aching pain; burning pain; dull pain; sensations of generalized or localized pain; stabbing pain

title: Other specified sensory functions and pain
Voice functions
definition: Functions of the production of various sounds by the passage of air through the larynx.
exclusions: Alternative vocalization functions; Articulation functions; Mental functions of language

title: Other specified voice and speech functions
Exercise tolerance functions
definition: Functions related to respiratory and cardiovascular capacity as required for enduring physical exertion.
exclusions: additional respiratory functions; Functions of the cardiovascular system; Haematological system functions; Respiration functions; Respiratory muscle functions

title: Other specified functions of the cardiovascular, haematological, immunological and respiratory systems
Functions of the cardiovascular, haematological, immunological and respiratory systems, unspecified
Functions related to the digestive system
Other specified functions of the digestive, metabolic and endocrine systems
Functions of the digestive, metabolic and endocrine systems, unspecified
Urination functions
definition: Functions of discharge of urine from the urinary bladder.
exclusions: Sensations associated with urinary functions; Urinary excretory functions

title: Sexual functions
definition: Mental and physical functions related to the sexual act, including the arousal, preparatory, orgasmic and resolution stages.
exclusions: Procreation functions; Sensations associated with genital and reproductive functions

title: Other specified genitourinary and reproductive functions
Mobility of joint functions
definition: Functions of the range and ease of movement of a joint.
exclusions: Control of voluntary movement functions; Stability of joint functions

title: Muscle power functions
definition: Functions related to the force generated by the contraction of a muscle or muscle groups.
exclusions: Functions of structures adjoining the eye; Muscle endurance functions; Muscle tone functions

title: Other specified neuromusculoskeletal and movement-related functions
Other specified functions of the skin and related structures
Functions of the skin and related structures, unspecified
Solving problems
definition: Finding solutions to questions or situations by identifying and analysing issues, developing options and solutions, evaluating potential effects of solutions, and executing a chosen solution, such as in resolving a dispute between two people.
exclusions: Making decisions; Thinking

title: Other specified learning and applying knowledge
Carrying out daily routine
definition: Carrying out simple or complex and coordinated actions in order to plan, manage and complete the requirements of day-to-day procedures or duties, such as budgeting time and making plans for separate activities throughout the day.
exclusions: Undertaking multiple tasks

title: Handling stress and other psychological demands
definition: Carrying out simple or complex and coordinated actions to manage and control the psychological demands required to carry out tasks demanding significant responsibilities and involving stress, distraction, or crises, such as taking exams, driving a vehicle during heavy traffic, finishing a task within a time-limit or taking responsibility for a group of individuals.

title: Other specified general tasks and demands
Communicating with - receiving - spoken messages
definition: Comprehending literal and implied meanings of messages in spoken language, such as understanding that a statement asserts a fact or is an idiomatic expression.

title: Conversation
definition: Starting, sustaining and ending an interchange of thoughts and ideas, carried out by means of spoken, written, signed or other forms of language, with one or more people one knows or who are strangers, in formal or casual settings.

title: Maintaining a standing position
definition: Staying in a standing position for some time such as when standing in a queue.
inclusions: staying in a standing position or hard surfaces; staying in a standing position on a slope; staying in a standing position on slippery surfaces

title: Transferring oneself
definition: Moving from one surface to another, such as sliding along a bench or moving from a bed to a chair, without changing body position.
exclusions: Changing basic body position

title: Walking
definition: Moving along a surface on foot, step by step, so that one foot is always on the ground, such as when strolling, sauntering, walking forwards, backwards, or sideways.
exclusions: Moving around; Transferring oneself

title: Moving around within the home
definition: Walking and moving around in one's home, within a room, between rooms, and around the whole residence or living area.
inclusions: moving from floor to floor; moving on an attached balcony; moving in a restricted area such as a courtyard, porch or garden

title: Moving around using equipment
definition: Moving the whole body from place to place, on any surface or space, by using specific devices designed to facilitate moving or create other ways of moving around, such as with skates, skis, or scuba equipment, or moving down the street in a self-propelled wheelchair or a walker.
exclusions: Driving; Moving around; Transferring oneself; Using transportation; Walking

title: Using transportation
definition: Using transportation to move around as a passenger, such as being driven in a car, bus, rickshaw, jitney, pram or stroller, wheelchair, animal-powered vehicle, private or public taxi, train, tram, subway, boat or aircraft and using humans for transportation.
exclusions: Driving; Moving around using equipment

title: Washing oneself
definition: Washing and drying one's whole body, or body parts, using water and appropriate cleaning and drying materials or methods, such as bathing, showering, washing hands and feet, face and hair, and drying with a towel.
exclusions: Caring for body parts; Toileting

title: Caring for body parts
definition: Looking after those parts of the body, such as skin, face, teeth, scalp, nails and genitals, that require more than washing and drying.
exclusions: Toileting; Washing oneself

title: Toileting
definition: Planning and carrying out the elimination of human waste (menstruation, urination and defecation), and clean oneself afterwards.
exclusions: Caring for body parts; Dressing; Moving around within the home; Walking; Washing oneself

title: Dressing
definition: Carrying out the coordinated actions and tasks of putting on and taking off clothes and footwear in sequence and in keeping with climatic and social conditions, such as by putting on, adjusting and removing shirts, skirts, blouses, pants, undergarments, saris, kimono, tights, hats, gloves, coats, shoes, boots, sandals and slippers.

title: Eating
definition: Carrying out the coordinated tasks and actions of eating food that has been served, bringing it to the mouth and consuming it in culturally acceptable ways, cutting or breaking food into pieces, opening containers and packets, using eating implements, having meals, feasting or dining.
exclusions: Drinking; Preparing meals

title: Looking after one's health
definition: Ensuring physical comfort, health and physical and mental well-being, such as by maintaining a balanced diet, and an appropriate level of physical activity, keeping warm or cool, avoiding harms to health, following safe sex practices, such as using condoms, getting immunizations and regular physical examinations.

title: Preparing meals
definition: Planning, organizing, cooking and serving simple and complex meals for oneself and others, such as by making a menu, selecting edible food and drink, getting together ingredients for preparing meals, cooking with heat and preparing cold foods and drinks, and serving the food.
exclusions: Acquisition of goods and services; Caring for household objects; caring for others; Doing housework; Drinking; Eating

title: Doing housework
definition: Managing a household by cleaning the house, washing clothes, using household appliances, storing food and disposing of garbage, such as by sweeping, mopping, washing counters, walls and other surfaces~ collecting and disposing of household garbage~ tidying rooms, closets and drawers~ collecting, washing, drying, folding and ironing clothes~ cleaning footwear~ using brooms, brushes and vacuum cleaners~ using washing machines, dryers and irons.
exclusions: Acquiring a place to live; Acquisition of goods and services; Caring for household objects; caring for others; Preparing meals

title: Assisting others
definition: Assisting household members and others with their learning, communicating, self-care, movement, within the house or outside~ being concerned about the well-being of household members and others.
exclusions: Remunerative employment

title: Basic interpersonal interactions
definition: Interacting with people in a contextually and socially appropriate manner, such as by showing consideration and esteem when appropriate, or responding to the feelings of others.

title: Relating with strangers
definition:
Engaging in temporary contacts and links with strangers for specific purposes, such as asking for directions or making a purchase.
Engaging in temporary contacts and links with strangers for specific purposes, such as when asking for information, directions or making a purchase.

title: Intimate relationships
definition: Creating and maintaining close or romantic relationships between individuals, such as husband and wife, lovers or sexual partners.

title: Other specified interpersonal interactions and relationships
Remunerative employment
definition: Engaging in all aspects of work, as an occupation, trade, profession or other form of employment, for payment, as an employee, full or part time, or self-employed, such as seeking employment and getting a job, doing the required tasks of the job, attending work on time as required, supervising other workers or being supervised, and performing required tasks alone or in groups.

title: Recreation and leisure
definition: Engaging in any form of play, recreational or leisure pursuit, such as informal or organized play and sports, programmes of physical fitness, relaxation, amusement or diversion, going to art galleries, museums, cinemas or theatres~ engaging in crafts or hobbies, reading or singing for enjoyment, playing musical instruments~ sightseeings, tourism and travelling for pleasure.
exclusions: Political life and citizenship; Religion and spirituality; remunerative and non-remunerative work ( and ); Riding animals for transportation; Reading; Singing

title: Human rights
definition: Enjoying all nationally and internationally recognized rights that are accorded to people by virtue of their humanity alone, such as human rights as recognized by the United Nations Universal Declaration of Human Rights (1948), the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child (1989), the United Nations Standard Rules for the Equalization of Opportunities for Persons with Disabilities (1993), and the United Nations Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (2006)~ the right to self-determination or autonomy~ and the right to control over one's destiny.
exclusions: Political life and citizenship

title: Other specified community, social and civic life
Community, social and civic life, unspecified
Grade I
definition: Well differentiated

title: Grade II
definition: Moderately differentiated

title: Grade III
definition: Poorly differentiated

title: Grade IV
definition: Undifferentiated

title: Undetermined grade
definition: Grade cannot be assessed

title: NYHA Class I - No limitation of physical activity
definition: No limitation of physical activity. Ordinary physical activity does not cause undue fatigue, palpitation, dyspnoea (shortness of breath).

title: NYHA Class II - Slight limitation of physical activity
definition: Slight limitation of physical activity. Comfortable at rest. Ordinary physical activity results in fatigue, palpitation, dyspnoea (shortness of breath).

title: NYHA Class III - Marked limitation of physical activity
definition: Marked limitation of physical activity. Comfortable at rest. Less than ordinary activity causes fatigue, palpitation or dyspnoea.

title: NYHA Class IV - Unable to carry on any physical activity without discomfort
definition: Unable to carry on any physical activity without discomfort. Symptoms of heart failure at rest. If any physical activity is undertaken, discomfort increases.

title: Stage I: Asymptomatic, incomplete blood vessel obstruction
Stage IIA: Claudication at a distance > 200 metres
Stage IIB: Claudication at a distance < 200 metres
Stage III: Rest pain, mostly in the feet
Stage IV: Necrosis and/or gangrene of the limb
Grade 0 Category 0: Asymptomatic -no hemodynamically significant occlusive disease
definition: Objective criteria: Normal treadmill or reactive hyperemia test

title: Grade 0 Category 1: Mild claudication
definition: Objective criteria: Completes treadmill exercise~ ankle pressure (AP) after exercise > 50 mm Hg but at least 20 mm Hg lower than resting value

title: Grade I Category 2: Moderate claudication
definition: Objective criteria: Between categories 1 and 3

title: Grade I Category 3: Severe claudication
definition: Objective criteria: Cannot complete standard treadmill exercise, and ankle pressure (AP) after exercise < 50 mm Hg

title: Grade II Category 4: Ischaemic rest pain
definition: Objective criteria: Resting ankle pressure (AP) < 40 mm Hg, flat or barely pulsatile ankle or metatarsal pulse volume recording (PVR)~ toe pressure (TP) < 30 mm Hg

title: Grade III Category 5: Minor tissue loss - nonhealing ulcer, focal gangrene with diffuse pedal ischaemia
definition: Objective criteria: Resting ankle pressure (AP) < 60 mm Hg, ankle or metatarsal pulse volume recording (PVR) flat or barely pulsatile~ toe pressure (TP) < 40 mm Hg

title: Grade III Category 6: Major tissue loss - extending above transmetatarsal (TM) level, functional foot no longer salvageable
definition: Objective criteria: Same as category 5.

Anaphylaxis grade 1
definition: Anaphylaxis grade 1 is considered when there is a single organ system involved.

title: Anaphylaxis grade 2
definition: Anaphylaxis grade 2 is considered in cases of more than one organ system involvement~ but not life-threatening.

title: Anaphylaxis grade 3
definition: Anaphylaxis grade 3 is considered in life-threatening cases with more than one organ system involved.

title: Anaphylaxis grade 4
definition: Anaphylaxis grade 4 is considered in life-threatening cases with cardiac arrest.

title: Cause of late effect
definition: A condition that results in a late effect.

title: Rapid onset
definition: Rapid or sudden onset of a condition/illness that requires more immediate and/or aggressive treatment

title: Pregnancy
definition: For the mother, the period of time between fertilization and parturition.

title: Puerperium
definition: For the mother, the period of time that lasts from birth through the 42nd day of life for the child.

title: Early Neonatal
definition: The period of time from birth through the seventh day of life.

title: Late Neonatal
definition: The period of time from the eighth through the 28th day of life.

title: Perinatal
definition: The period of time between 22 weeks after fertilization and 7 days after parturition.

title: Infancy
definition: The period of time between 29 and 365 days of life.

title: Child under 5
definition: The period of time from the start of the 1st year of life through the end of the 4th.

title: Middle Childhood
definition: The period of time from the start of the 5th year of life through the end of the 10th.

title: Early Adolescence
definition: The period of time from the start of the 11th year of life through the end of the 14th

title: Middle Adolescence
definition: The period of time from the start of the 15th year of life through the end of the 17th.

title: Late Adolescence
definition: The period of time from the start of the 18th year of life through the end of the 19th.

title: Young Adult
definition: The period of time from the start of the 20th year of life through the end of the 24th.

title: Adult
definition: The period of time from the start of the 25th year of life through the end of the 64th.

title: Early Geriatric
definition: The period of time from the start of the 65th year of life through the end of the 84th year

title: Late Geriatric
definition: The period of time from the start of the 85th year through the end of life.

title: Duration of pregnancy less than 5 completed weeks
Duration of pregnancy more than 36 completed weeks
Unspecified duration of pregnancy
definition: Duration of pregnancy not specified

title: Ageing-related
definition: Ageing-related means "caused by biological processes which persistently lead to the loss of organism's adaptation and progress in older ages"

title: Streptococcus pneumoniae
definition: This is a Gram-positive, alpha-hemolytic, aerotolerant anaerobic member of the genus Streptococcus. This diagnosis is as the cause of diseases classified to other chapters.

title: Zika virus
definition: Zika virus (ZIKV) is a flavivirus from the Flaviviridae family and Spondweni serocomplex. The virus was first identified in 1947 in the Zika forest in Uganda in the rhesus macaque population. There are two main lineages of ZIKV, the African lineage and the Asian lineage.

title: Influenza A H1pdm09N2 2:6 reassortant influenza virus
Human respiratory syncytial virus
definition: This is a virus that causes respiratory tract infections. It is a major cause of lower respiratory tract infections and hospital visits during infancy and childhood. This diagnosis is the cause of diseases classified to other chapters.

title: Monkeypox virus Clade I
definition: Based on the virus genome sequence there are two clades/variants of the Monkeypox virus recognised~ Clade I and Clade II.

title: Monkeypox virus Clade IIa
definition: Clade IIa and Clade IIb are two recognised subclades of the Monkeypox virus Clade II.

title: Monkeypox virus Clade IIb
definition:
Clade IIa and Clade IIb are two recognised subclades of the Monkeypox virus Clade II.
Clade IIb refers to a group of variants largely circulating in the 2022 global outbreak.

title: Reovirus
definition: Reovirus is a nonenveloped double-stranded RNA virus. This virus was initially not known to be related to any specific disease, and so was named Respiratory Enteric Orphan virus. However, some members of the reovirus family have been shown to cause mild illnesses such as diarrhea.

title: Aspergillus terreus
definition: The Aspergillus terreus is found in a wide variety of habitats, soil, compost, or dust, and the spectrum of diseases caused covers allergic bronchopulmonary aspergillosis, Aspergillus bronchitis and/or tracheobronchitis, and invasive and disseminated aspergillosis.

title: Talaromyces marneffei
definition: The fungus formerly known as Penicillium marneffei

title: Coccidia
definition: subclass of microscopic, spore-forming, single-celled obligate intracellular parasites belonging to the apicomplexan class Conoidasida

title: Lymphocytes
definition: Lymphocytes include natural killer cells (which function in cell-mediated, cytotoxic innate immunity), T cells (for cell-mediated, cytotoxic adaptive immunity), and B cells (for humoral, antibody-driven adaptive immunity).

title: Platelet-rich plasma
definition: Platelet-rich plasma consists of two elements: plasma (the liquid portion of blood) and platelets (a type of blood cells that play an important role in healing throughout the body).

title: Connective and other soft tissues of thymus
Craniopharyngeal duct
definition: Craniopharyngeal duct is a bony channel that connects the floor of the sella turcica, along the midline, to the nasopharynx.

Biocompatibility
definition: Problem associated with undesirable local or systemic effects due to exposure to medical device materials or leachates from those materials by a patient who has an implant or is receiving treatment with a device made from them.

title: Device appears to trigger rejection
definition: The device appears to elicit undesired response in the patient to the presence of an implanted or invasive device, without inherent device failure, e.g. fibrous encapsulation, or inflammation of the tissue around the device, or extrusion of the device

title: Inadequacy of device shape or size
definition: The physical size or shape of the device was inadequate with regard to the patient's anatomy.

title: Failure to osseointegrate
definition: Problem associated with the failure to see direct anchorage of an implant by the formation of bony tissue around the implant without the growth of fibrous tissue at the bone-implant interface.

title: Loss of osseointegration
definition: Problem associated with weakened integration of the device at the bone-implant interface due to loss of fibrous and/or bony tissue and leading to compromised anchorage of the device

title: Loosening of implant not related to bone-ingrowth
definition: Problem associated with the loss of direct anchorage of an implanted device over time or due to an injury.

title: Migration of device
definition: Problem with all or part of an implanted or invasive device moving from its intended location within the body.

title: Expulsion of device
definition: Problem with all or part of an implanted or invasive device being completely expelled from its intended location within the body.

title: Dull or blunt
definition: Problem associated with a device not being as sharp as intended or expected.

title: Nonstandard device
definition: Problem associated with the device that does not meet the specifications or requirements for which it was manufactured (e.g. materials, parts, manufacturing process).

title: Difficult to open or remove packaging material
definition: Problem associated with difficulty for users to operate the device, specifically as it relates to the opening or removal of the outer wrapping.

title: Incomplete or missing packaging
definition: Problem associated with the nonconformance to the device specifications due to incomplete or missing packaging that may compromise the device operation as intended.

title: Unsealed device packaging
definition: Problem associated with the loss of packaging seal.

title: Tear, rip or hole in device packaging
definition: Problem associated with packaging damage (tear, rip or hole) prior to the use of the device.

title: Component misassembled
definition: A device found to have one or more components incorrectly assembled when delivered to the user facility.

title: Component missing
definition: A device component(s) found to be missing when delivered to the user facility.

title: Delivered as unsterile product
definition: Problem associated with a device being received in such a manner to indicate that its sterility has been compromised (e.g. sterile packaging breached, visible contaminate present)

title: Device emits odour
definition: Problem associated with an unexpected or inappropriate smell released by the device.

title: Clumping in device or device ingredient
definition: Problem associated with the aggregation of particles into irregular masses.

title: Coagulation in device or device ingredient
definition: Problem associated with the undesired characterization of congealing, solidifying, thickening, curdling.

title: Precipitate in device or device ingredient
definition: Problem associated with the separation of solid particles from a liquid as the result of a chemical or physical change.

title: Cross reactivity
definition: Problem associated with the degree to which an antibody or antigen participates in cross reactions.

title: Particulates
definition: Substances that consist of separate particles that are introduced by the device during use.

title: High pH
definition: pH higher than expected and / or anticipated.

title: Low pH
definition: pH lower than expected and / or anticipated.

title: Fracture of device
definition: Problem associated with a partial or full-thickness crack in the device materials.

title: Loss of or failure to bond
definition: Problem associated with lack or loss of adherence between materials intended to be joined together by an adhesive.

title: Material fragmentation
definition: Problem associated with small pieces of the device breaking off unexpectedly.

title: Solder joint fracture
definition: Problem associated with undesired damage or breakage in a solder joint of materials used in the device construction.

title: Burst container or vessel
definition: Problem associated with the pressure inside a vessel or container rising to such a degree that the container ruptures.

title: Explosion
definition: Problem associated with the violent bursting due to the sudden expansion of air, gas or fluid.

title: Crack
definition: Problem associated with an undesired partial separation and/or a visible opening along the length or width in the materials that are used in the device construction.

title: Calcified
definition: Problem associated with buildup of calcium salts on the device.

title: Corroded
definition: Problem associated with the chemical or electrochemical reaction between materials, usually a metal and its environment that produces a deterioration of the metal and its properties.

title: Material erosion
definition: Problem associated with a progressive loss of a material from a solid surface.

title: Pitted
definition: Problem associated with the corrosion of a material's surface, confined to a point or small area that takes the form of cavities.

title: Flaked
definition: Problem associated with the detachment of small pieces of the coating film of a material.

title: Peeled or delaminated
definition: Peeling or delamination of composite materials, including coatings, that occurs when layers are separated as a result of stress or impact and resulting in loss of mechanical toughness.

title: Naturally worn
definition: Problem associated with material damage to a surface, usually involving progressive loss or displacement of material, due to relative motion between that surface and a contacting substance or substances.

title: Unraveled material
definition: Problem due to the undesired unravelling of material (e.g. disentangled, unwound etc.).

title: Deformation due to compressive stress
definition: Problem associated with an undesired bulge, bend, bow, kink, or wavy condition observed in the device material resulting from compressive stresses.

title: Dent in material
definition: Problem associated with a undesired change in shape, characterised by the presence of a slight hollow (dent) in the device surface.

title: Failure to fold
definition: Problem associated with an undesired material change in physical property, characterised by failure to fold.

title: Failure to unfold or unwrap
definition: Problem associated with the comprising materials' deformation in that device fails to open its wrapping or open/extend in a certain manner i.e. balloon or lens.

title: Material frayed
definition: Problem associated with the comprising materials having damaged edges.

title: Material invagination
definition: Problem associated with an undesired material change in shape, characterised by the infolding of one part within another part of a structure.

title: Material too rigid or stiff
definition: Problem associated with an undesired material change in physical property, characterised by rigidity (it resists deformation in response to an applied force).

title: Material too soft or flexible
definition: Problem associated with any device material that results in the material's inability to maintain the desired shape or support function.

title: Material twisted or bent
definition: Problem associated with deformations that lead to twisting or bending of the device.

title: Melted
definition: Problem associated with a solid device being transformed into a molten or liquid state.

title: Stretched
definition: Problem associated with an increase or elongation in a materials' dimension.

title: Material discoloured
definition: Problem associated with an undesired streak, pattern and/or a noticeable change in color from the rest of the materials used in the device construction.

title: Material disintegration
definition: Problem associated with material breaking into small particles.

title: Material opacification
definition: Problem associated with an undesirable opaqueness or cloudiness.

title: Material puncture or hole
definition: Device material(s) punctured leading to undesired holes/openings.

title: Material protrusion or extrusion
definition: Problem associated with undesired physical appearance of device material, specifically when material extends beyond or above device surface.

title: Material rupture
definition: Problem associated with perforations that lead to bursting of the device.

title: Material separation
definition: Problem associated with an undesired disassociation or breaking apart of the device.

title: Material split, cut or torn
definition: Problem associated with materials composing the device being split, cut or torn due to external forces (e.g. wrenching or laceration) or internal forces (e.g. exceeding the tensile stress limits belonging to the materials used in the device construction).

title: Scratched material
definition: Problem associated with an undesirable shallow cut or narrow groove in the surface of the device materials.

title: Detachment of device or device component
definition: Problem associated with the separation of the device from its physical construct, integrity, or chassis.

title: Device damaged by another device
definition: Problem associated with one device causing harm to another device.

title: Failure to eject
definition: Problem associated with the inability to remove or discharge the device from the location of use.

title: Unintended ejection
definition: Problem associated with unexpected discharge of the device from expected location includes but not limited to the device such as clip appliers, film cartridge, staples.

title: Fluid leak
definition: Escape (Release, Discharge) of fluid through an unintended location - often accompanied by a loss of pressure and/or output.

title: Gas leak
definition: Problem associated with the unintended escape of a gas from the container in which it is housed.

title: Gel leak
definition: Escape (Release, Discharge) of gel through an unintended location - as in leakage of ultrasound gel. Escape or release of gel from containment structures - as in gel filled implant leak.

title: Radiation leak
definition: Escape of radiation (energy in the form of waves or subatomic particles, especially those that cause ionization) through containment structures, leading to unintended exposure.

title: Perivalvular leak
definition: Problem associated with the escape of blood around a heart valve, particularly around its leaflets.

title: Failure to fire
definition: Problem associated with failure of the device to discharge its load (e.g. surgical stapler failed to partially or completely deploy its staples).

title: Misfire
definition: Problem associated with a therapy or algorithm not being delivered or executed at the expected time.

title: Mechanical jam
definition: The motion of the device is prevented or restricted.

title: Failure to align
definition: Problem associated with a circuit, equipment, or system whereby its functions fail to be properly synchronized or its relative positions properly oriented.

title: Failure to cut
definition: Inability of the device to make an incision, pierce or open as intended.

title: Failure to cycle
definition: Problem associated with the device failing to complete a series of processes or events.

title: Failure to form staple
definition: Problem associated with the device failing to connect tissue with a stapling device due to the staples not forming correctly.

title: Noise, audible
definition: Problem associated with any unintended sound which emanates from the device (for example, squeaking from two parts rubbing together or buzzing sounds from electrical components).

title: Physical resistance or sticking
definition: Problem associated with the lack of movement in the device due parts sticking or seizing.

title: Retraction problem
definition: Problem associated with drawing back the device to an intended location.

title: Structural collapse
definition: Problem associated with the buckling or crushing of material from external forces.

title: Sharp edges
definition: The device has undesirable sharp edges which can cause harm or damage.

title: Difficult to fold or unfold
definition: Problem associated with the use of the device in terms of user experiencing difficulty to close or to spread out/extend length of the device, even if the operation is being performed according to labeled instructions for use.

title: Difficult to open or close
definition: Problem associated with the use of the device in terms of user experiencing difficulty opening and closing the device, even if the operation is being performed according to labeled instructions for use.

title: Incomplete coaptation
definition: Problem associated with the heart valve leaflet not closing properly.

title: Device dislodged or dislocated
definition: Problems associated with the device not remaining in an expected location.

title: Device tipped over
definition: Problem associated with the inability of the device to stay in an upright position.

title: Device fell
definition: Problem associated with the device or a component unexpectedly being dropped or moving down from an intended place.

title: Device slipped
definition: Problem associated with the device moving or sliding from the intended position.

title: Unintended collision of device
definition: Problem associated with the device impacting with another object.

title: Unintended system motion
definition: Problem associated with any motion of the system or components that was not initiated by the user.

title: Unstable device
definition: Problem associated with the mechanical stability of the device.

title: Vibration of device
definition: Problem associated with the undesirable mechanical oscillation.

title: Misfocusing
definition: The problem relates to the poor focusing of the object or the focus is on the wrong object or in the wrong area.

title: Optical decentration
definition: Problem associated with being off-center of optical lenses.

title: Optical discolouration
definition: Problem associated with an undesired change of color.

title: Optical distortion
definition: Problem associated with an optical defect in an image-forming system whereby the image is not the shape of an ideal image of the object.

title: Optical obstruction
definition: Problem associated with the blocking of optical devices, e.g. visual pathways.

title: Inappropriate audible prompt or feedback
definition: Problem with audible messages which do not guide a device user to the correct action.

title: Inaudible or unclear audible prompt or feedback
definition: Problem associated with audible prompts which cannot be heard clearly.

title: No audible prompt or feedback
definition: Problem associated with the device ceasing to provide audible prompts.

title: Device displays incorrect message
definition: Problem associated with providing incorrect display information.

title: Display difficult to read
definition: Problem associated with legibility of the display, compromising for instance the reading/interpretation of patient parameters or test results. Legibility problems can be due to color, size of font, display screen contrast or other factors.

title: Erratic or intermittent display
definition: A device does not consistently display the same message, result, reading, or image. E.g. the display might flicker, switch between readings or messages, or go completely blank for brief periods of time.

title: Image display error or artifact
definition: Problem with image display leading to corrupted images or readouts/measurement indications.

title: Image orientation incorrect
definition: Problem associated with an incorrect image orientation on the device display.

title: No display or image
definition: Problem associated with the absence of display or image.

title: No visual prompts or feedback
definition: Problem associated with the device ceasing to provide visual feedback.

title: Poor quality image
definition: Inadequate quality of an image or any visual representation displayed by the device, or output from the device.

title: Visual prompts will not clear
definition: Problem with visual messages which continue to be displayed on / by the device after the appropriate action has been taken.

title: Inappropriate tactile prompt or feedback
definition: Problem with tactile feedback which does not guide a device user to the correct action.

title: No tactile prompts or feedback
definition: Problem associated with the device ceasing to provide tactile feedback.

title: Energy spectrum incorrect
definition: Problem associated with the energy output from the device not being in the expected part of the spectrum.

title: Failure to deliver energy
definition: Problem associated with the failure of the device to deliver any energy.

title: Intermittent energy output
definition: Problem associated with the energy output from the device being inconsistent over time.

title: Output above specifications
definition: Device output is exceeding the documented specifications of the device.

title: Output below specifications
definition: Device output is below the documented specifications of the device.

title: Therapeutic or diagnostic output failure
definition: Problem associated with the failure of the device to deliver the output required for treatment or identification of a disease.

title: Therapy delivered to incorrect body area
definition: Problem associated with the device causing unintended therapeutic action to an area of the body other than the intended area.

title: Radiation output failure
definition: Problem associated with the absence of radiation output from radiological or diagnostic devices.

title: Radiation overexposure
definition: Problem associated with excessive radiation emitted from radiological or diagnostic devices.

title: Radiation underexposure
definition: Problem associated with too little radiation emitted from radiological or diagnostic devices.

title: Unexpected or unintended radiation output
definition:
Device-emitted radiation when it was not supposed to. This applies to devices which are intended to emit radiation, and the radiation being emitted from the correct part of the device, but at an incorrect time.
Use "radiation leak" if the device emits radiation which should never have been emitted, or from a location from which it should never be emitted.
exclusions: Radiation leak

title: Gas output problem
definition: Problem associated with gas output.

title: No device output
definition: Problem associated with no measurement outcome, value or data obtained from the device.

title: Signal artifact
definition: Problem associated with impurities or interference in a signal (e.g. ECG artifact).

title: Failure to obtain sample
definition: The device does not collect or transfer the sample.

title: False negative result
definition: Problem associated with the device incorrectly reporting that something has not been detected and may mislead the operator into not taking certain actions when action should be taken.

title: False positive result
definition: Problem associated with the device incorrectly reporting that something has been detected and may mislead the operator to take certain actions.

title: Incorrect measurement
definition: Measurement obtained from or provided by the device is obviously incorrect.

title: Nonreproducible results
definition: Device results cannot be reliably reproduced.

title: High readings
definition: Reading provided by the device is too high or higher than expected.

title: Low readings
definition: Reading provided by the device is too low or lower than expected.

title: High test results
definition: Test results provided by the device are too high or higher than expected.

title: Low test results
definition: Test results provided by the device are too low or lower than expected.

title: Unable to obtain readings
definition: The device does not provide or display a valid reading.

title: Missing test results
definition: Problem associated with the results of a test or measurement not appearing.

title: Unexpected therapeutic results
definition: Problem associated with the use of the device for therapeutic purposes.

title: Failure to capture
definition: Problem associated with the failure to achieve effective and consistent depolarization of the heart resulting from the electrical stimulus of the pacemaker.

title: High capture threshold
definition: Problem with the amount of output energy needed to cause cardiac depolarization being higher than expected/desired.

title: Intermittent capture
definition: Problem associated with the ineffective and inconsistent depolarization of the heart.

title: Unstable capture threshold
definition: Problem with the amount of output energy needed to cause cardiac depolarization being unstable.

title: Continuous firing
definition: Problem associated with the excessive production of electrical impulses over a period.

title: Arcing at paddles
definition: Problem associated with electrical current flowing through a gap between paddles (conductive surfaces), typically resulting in a visible flash of light.

title: Arcing of electrodes
definition: Problem associated with electrical current flowing through a gap between electrodes (conductive surfaces), typically resulting in a visible flash of light.

title: Sparking
definition: Problem associated with a flash of light related to an electrical discharge into a normally non conductive medium, such as air. Not associated with a discharge between two conductive surfaces.

title: Battery problem with high impedance
definition: Problem related to increased battery internal impedance.

title: Battery problem with low impedance
definition: Problem related to decreased battery internal impedance.

title: Failure to run on battery
definition: Problem associated with the device failing to operate when not connected to a fixed power source.

title: Premature discharge of battery
definition: Battery discharging earlier than expected.

title: Aborted charge
definition: Problem associated with the premature ending of the charging process (e.g. of a battery or other charge storage device).

title: Delayed charge time
definition: Problem associated with an unexpected amount of time required to charge the device (e.g. a delay in starting charging or a longer than expected charge time).

title: Failure to charge
definition: Problem associated with inability to initiate the appropriate charging process (e.g. of a battery or other charge storage device)

title: Failure to discharge
definition: Problem associated with the failure of a battery or other charge storage device to appropriately discharge as intended. Does not apply to defibrillation.

title: Complete loss of power
definition: Problem associated with the lack of power to run the device.

title: Intermittent loss of power
definition: Problem associated with an intermittent disruption to the power to run the device.

title: Failure to power up
definition: Problem associated with the inability of the device to turn on related to energy delivered to the device.

title: Unintended power up
definition: Problem associated with the device turning on when not intended.

title: Decreased sensitivity
definition: Problem with the device being less sensitive to an input than intended or expected.

title: Increased sensitivity
definition: Problem with the device being more sensitive to an input than intended or expected.

title: Failure to analyze signal
definition: Problem with the device not analyzing a signal.

title: Failure to select signal
definition: Problem associated with the failure of the device to select the appropriate input signal.

title: High sensing threshold
definition: Problem associated with the amount of input required by the device to detect a signal being higher than expected/desired.

title: Low sensing threshold
definition: Problem associated with the amount of an input required by the device to detect a signal being lower than expected/desired.

title: Loss of threshold
definition: Problem associated with the loss of the minimum amount of energy, voltage, or current needed to consistently stimulate the heart muscle.

title: Failure to sense
definition: Problem associated with the failure of the device designed to respond to a physical stimulus (as temperature, illumination, motion) to transmit a resulting signal for interpretation or measurement.

title: Over-sensing
definition: Problem related to failure of the device to properly filter cardiac signals resulting in inappropriate device response.

title: Under-sensing
definition: Problem related to failure of the device to properly detect intrinsic cardiac activity and respond appropriately.

title: Sensing intermittently
definition: Problem with the device receiving an incoming signal on an intermittent basis when expected to be continuous.

title: Incorrect interpretation of signal
definition: Problem with the device inappropriately analyzing a signal.

title: Failure to conduct
definition: Problem associated with the inability of the device to allow a current of electricity to pass or to conduct electricity continuously along an electrical path.

title: Difficult to interrogate
definition: Problem associated with difficulty of a transponder system to trigger a response.

title: Failure to interrogate
definition: Problem associated with the device failure to appropriately respond to signals from a system designed to interrogate its status.

title: Failure to convert rhythm
definition: Failure of the device therapy or set of therapies to terminate the harmful cardiac rhythm that the therapy is meant to terminate.

title: Inaccurate synchronization
definition: Problem associated with an error due to imperfect timing of two operations, e.g. signal transmission time.

title: Inappropriate waveform
definition: Failure of the device to generate a correctly-shaped pacing output, e.g., a waveform that is too wide.

title: No pacing
definition: Problem associated with the device ceasing to deliver paces.

title: Pacemaker found in back-up mode
definition: A device with a pacing function found in back-up Mode. This may be an appropriate fail-safe action (e.g. end of battery life), or be caused by device-malfunction or due to operator error.

title: Pacing asynchronously
definition: Problem associated with a pacing transmission process such that between any two significant instants in the same group, there is always an integral number of unit intervals. Between two significant instants located in different groups, there are not always an integral number of unit intervals.

title: Pacing inadequately
definition: Pacing voltage or pulse width is less than desired.

title: Pacing intermittently
definition: Problem associated with the failure of pacing device for a limited period of time, following which the item recovers its ability to perform its required function without being subjected to any external corrective action. Note: such a failure is often recurrent.

title: Pocket stimulation
definition: Problem associated with a pocket of skin in which the pulse generator is housed.

title: Failure to deliver shock
definition: Problem associated with the failure of the device to deliver electrical energy intended to change an electrical rhythm.

title: Inappropriate shock
definition: Problem associated with the inappropriate delivery of an electrical energy.

title: Intermittent shock
definition: Problem associated with the failure to deliver shock for a limited period of time, following which the item recovers its ability to perform its required function without being subjected to any external corrective action. Note: such a failure is often recurrent.

title: Unintended electrical shock
definition: The device delivers unintended electrical shock.

title: Grounding malfunction
definition: Problem associated with the inability to connect conductors of an electronic system for the purpose of controlling or impeding ground currents and voltages.

title: Electrical overstress
definition: Problem associated with an electrical activity that exceeded the specified threshold limit of the internal integrated circuitry.

title: Electro-static discharge
definition: Problem associated with the discharge of electricity between two bodies previously electrically charged.

title: Failure to shut off
definition: Problem associated with the device not powering off when a shutdown was requested.

title: Unexpected shutdown
definition: Problem associated with the device unexpectedly powering down.

title: Electromagnetic compatibility problem
definition: Problem associated with the ability of a system to function in its electromagnetic environment without introducing intolerable disturbances to anything in its environment.

title: Circuit failure
definition: Problem associated with a failure of the internal network paths or electrical circuitry (i.e. electrical components, circuit boards, wiring)

title: Impedance problem
definition: Problem associated with electrical impedance levels between device and patient connections.

title: Failure to calibrate
definition: Problem associated with the failure of the device to perform a self-calibration procedure or process designed to assure the accuracy and proper performance of the device.

title: Failure to recalibrate
definition: Problem associated with the failure of the device which is unable to regain a standard level of accuracy when performing a calibration procedure or process designed to assure the accuracy and proper performance of the device.

title: Imprecision
definition: Problem associated with the device providing imprecise measurements when compared to a reference standard.

title: Overcorrection
definition: Problem associated with an adjustment that surpasses a set of criteria.

title: Excessive cooling
definition: Problem associated with the device producing temperatures that are lower than specified.

title: Excessive heating
definition: Problem associated with the device having a warming or heating function, producing excessive heat.

title: Insufficient cooling
definition: Problem associated with the device insufficiently cooled in device active (working) or/and non-active (nonworking) state.

title: Insufficient heating
definition: Problem associated with the device or its components producing temperatures that are not as high as what is specified.

title: Overheating of device
definition: Problem associated with the device producing high temperatures, such that its operation is compromised or harm is caused (e.g. overheating that produces melting of components or automatic shutdown).

title: Thermal decomposition of device
definition: Problems associated with a discoloration or destruction as a result of thermal decomposition of the device.

title: Fire
definition: Problem associated with the combustion of the device with a steady flame.

title: Flare or flash
definition: Problem associated with device-related burn with an unsteady flame.

title: Smoke
definition: Problem associated with a cloud of vapor or gas generated from the device, generally associated after a fire or a burn.

title: Application network problem
definition: Problem associated with the deviations from documented system specifications that affects overall system performance and/or the performance of an individual device connected to that system.

title: Application program freezes, or becomes nonfunctional
definition: Problem associated with freezing and becoming nonfunctional of an application program.

title: Application program problem, dose calculation error
definition: Problem associated with the written program code or application software used by the device to calculate specific measurements or quantities managed by the device.

title: Application program problem, medication error
definition: Event in which the device software results in errors of medication preparation or administration.

title: Application program problem, parameter calculation error
definition: Problem associated with the written program code or application software used by the device to calculate parameters other than those related to dose or power.

title: Application program problem, power calculation error
definition: Problem associated with the written program code or application software used by the device for calculations related to device power.

title: Application program version or upgrade problem
definition: Problem associated with installing updates to a software system that affects the device performance or communication with another device.

title: Problem with software installation
definition: Problem associated with installing the device software in a manner that allows full functioning of the device. Source of installation could be manufacturer or user.

title: Unintended application program shutdown
definition: Problem associated with an unintended shutdown by malfunction of the application program.

title: Delayed program or algorithm execution
definition: Problem associated with delayed execution relating to program or algorithm.

title: Intermittent program or algorithm execution
definition: Problem associated with intermittent execution relating to program or algorithm.

title: Program or algorithm execution failure
definition: Problem associated with the failure of a program or algorithm to execute. Sudden/unexpected interruption to a program's execution.

title: Operating system becomes nonfunctional
definition: Problem associated with malfunction of the computer operating system as opposed to an application software problem.

title: Operating system version or upgrade problem
definition: Problem associated with replacing an older operating system to an up-to-date operating system.

title: Application security problem
definition: Problem associated with the acquisition of computer programming codes that can replicate and spread from one computer system to another thereby leading to damaged software, hardware and data.

title: Unauthorized access to computer system
definition: Problem associated with an access that was not permitted to the computer system that may lead to modification of program, corruption of data, or a break in network security. This concept is closely associated with computer integrity which is the degree to which a system or component prevents unauthorized access to, or modification of, computer programs or data.

title: Failure to back up
definition: Problem associated with the inability to back up or to retrieve a backed-up version (corrupted file) of device data or system files.

title: Failure to convert to back-up
definition: Problem associated with a failure to transition from a primary system, component, file, procedure to a backup in response to a failure in the primary item.

title: Loss of data
definition: Event in which data is unintentionally permanently or temporarily lost, deleted, corrupted, or overwritten.

title: Patient data problem
definition: Event in which data is accessed by the healthcare provider and either the wrong patient or the wrong data is retrieved despite correct inquiry procedures.

title: Date or time related software problem
definition: Problem associated with programming of calendar dates and/or time as a factor in the operation of the device.

title: Blocked connection
definition: Problem associated with linking of the device whereby their functional units set up to provide means for a transfer of fluid, gas, or data is prevented or impeded.

title: Decoupling
definition: Problem associated with the device being unassociated in such a way that fluid, gas, power or signal information may not be transferred from one to another.

title: Disconnection
definition: Problem associated with the linking of the device having a sufficient open space to prevent gas, liquid or electrical current flow between connectors.

title: Failure to disconnect
definition: Problem associated with the linking of the device whereby termination of the transfer of liquid, gas, electricity, or information cannot be accomplished, or linking components do not come apart, or disconnect, when expected.

title: Loose or intermittent connection
definition: Problem associated with the connection of the device being loose or intermittent.

title: Misconnection
definition: Problem associated with the connection of the device being improper or not in accordance with device specification, requirements or intended uses.

title: Incomplete or inadequate connection
definition: Problem associated with a partial linking of the device whereby device may appear to be connected however only a partial, intermittent or no transfer of liquid, gas, electricity, or information can be accomplished.

title: Fitting problem
definition: Problem associated with the connection of the device whereby channels, switching systems, and other functional units set up to provide means for a transfer of liquid, gas, electricity, or information do not match or fit.

title: Failure to read input signal
definition: Problem associated with a failure of the device to read a signal for interpretation or measurement.

title: Failure to transmit record
definition: Problem associated with a failure of the device to transmit a record for interpretation or measurement.

title: Intermittent communication failure
definition: Inconsistent or lack of intended communication of data among internal components or with other external devices.

title: Telemetry discrepancy
definition: Problem associated with variability of the transmission of telemetry signals.

title: Wireless communication problem
definition: Problems with the RF wireless technology characteristics and performance (e.g., frequency, output power, range, reception), wireless quality of service, wireless coexistence, security of wireless signals and data, and electromagnetic compatibility.

title: Deflation problem
definition: Problem associated with the inability of the device to release its contents.

title: Excess flow or over-infusion
definition: Problem associated with a delivery overdose of therapeutic agents, such as drugs or fluids being delivered into a device or a patient.

title: Inability to auto-fill
definition: Complete failure to fill as part of an automated process. For insufficient filling use "Short Fill". For excessive filling use "Overfill". For inconsistent filling use "Volume Accuracy Problem".

title: Overfill
definition: Excessive filling of a device
exclusions: Insufficient filling; Inconsistent filling

title: Short fill
definition: Insufficient filling of a device.
exclusions: Complete failure to fill; Inconsistent filling

title: Volume accuracy problem
definition: Inconsistent filling of a device. This describes a problem which is observed to vary between overfilling and under filling, and may be intermittent.
exclusions: Consistent overfilling; Consistent short filling

title: Inadequate filtration process
definition: Problem associated with the filter failing to remove items or substances which should have been removed.

title: Inadequate ultra filtration
definition: Problem associated with the transfer of fluid between the blood and dialysate through the dialysis membrane due to a pressure gradient (trans-membrane pressure) existing between the blood and dialysate compartments.

title: Backflow
definition: Continuous flow of fluid (e.g. liquid, gas) against the intended flow direction.

title: Free or unrestricted flow
definition: Problem associated with uncontrolled flow of infusion of air, gas or fluids.

title: Gradient increase
definition: Problem associated with the increased rate of change in temperature, pressure, or other variables as a function of distance, time, etc.

title: Inaccurate delivery
definition: Delivery at endpoint not as intended~ either too low or too high.

title: Inaccurate flow rate
definition: Problem associated with fluctuations in the flow volume delivered per time, even if end volume is correct, and delivered in the correct total time.

title: Intermittent infusion
definition: Problem associated with the infusion not being stable, characterised by intermittent stoppages to the flow.

title: Reflux with device
definition: Problem associated with partial backflow, compromising the device's flow output.

title: Restricted flow rate
definition: Problem associated with flow rate. Flow volume delivered over time is not reaching intended flow rate.

title: Tidal volume fluctuations
definition: Problem associated with the amount of gas that is inspired and expired during one respiratory cycle.

title: Inflation problem
definition: Problem associated with the inability of the device to expand or enlarge with the intended inflation agent (e.g. saline or air).

title: Insufficient flow or under infusion
definition: Problem associated with an insufficient dose of therapeutic agents, e.g., drugs or fluids being delivered into a patient under positive pressure.

title: Failure to deliver flow
