definition: Failure (=complete nonperformance) with regard to the intended function of delivery.

title: Failure to infuse
definition: Failure (=complete nonperformance) with regard to the intended function of infusion.

title: Inability to irrigate
definition: Failure (=complete nonperformance) with regard to the intended function of irrigation.

title: Complete blockage
definition: Problem related to an obstruction or blockage within the device component (e.g. tube, opening, pipe) that results in no flow.

title: Partial blockage
definition: Problem related to an obstruction or blockage within the device component (e.g. tube, opening, pipe) that results in a reduction of the flow rate.

title: Difficult to flush
definition: The device is difficult to flush, possibly indicating an obstruction within device.

title: Decrease in pressure
definition: Unintended decrease in pressure, compromising the device's intended function.

title: Increase in pressure of device
definition: Unintended increase in pressure, compromising the device's intended function.

title: No pressure
definition: Unintended complete loss of pressure, compromising the device's intended function.

title: Decreased pump speed
definition: Unintended decrease in pump speed and hence, probably, flow rate, compromising the intended function of the device.

title: Increased pump speed
definition: Unintended increase in pump speed and hence, probably, flow rate, compromising the intended function of the device.

title: Failure to pump
definition: Problem associated with the device which fails to start pumping.

title: Pumping stopped
definition: Unexpected or unintended cessation of pump.

title: Decrease in suction
definition: Problem associated with the removal of fluid or gas from a body cavity due to decreased suction.

title: Increase in suction
definition: Problem associated with the removal of excess fluid or gas from a body cavity due to increased suction.

title: Suction failure
definition: Problem associated with the complete inability to provide suction.

title: Failure to prime
definition: Problem associated with the device failing to begin the priming process (i.e. the process of preparation of device for the delivery of fluids).

title: Incomplete or inadequate priming
definition: Problem associated with not adequately preparing the device.

title: Activation failure or expansion failures
definition: Problem associated with the device failing to be activated including expansion.

title: Difficult or delayed activation
definition: Problem associated with delayed or difficult activation of the device.

title: Premature activation
definition: Problem associated with early and unexpected activation of the device.

title: Self-activation or keying
definition: Problem associated with the unintended activation of the device, or the device having been unexpectedly turned on during use.

title: Positioning failure
definition: Problem associated with the inability of the device to be positioned in a specified location.

title: Malposition of device
definition: Problem associated with the device being positioned in a location other than intended or specified.

title: Difficult or delayed positioning
definition: Problem associated with users experiencing difficulty or delay to position the device to a specified location.

title: Failure to advance
definition: Problem associated with failure to move the device to an intended location.

title: Difficult to advance
definition: Problem associated with difficulty moving the device to an intended location (e.g. difficulty in advancing guide wire).

title: Difficult to insert
definition: Problem associated with problems introducing or inserting the device, even if the user is operating the device in accordance with the instructions for use or labeling.

title: Difficult to remove
definition: Problem associated with the use of the device in terms of user experiencing difficulty to take out or get rid of the device, even if the user is operating device in accordance with the instructions for use or labeling.

title: Entrapment of device
definition: Problem associated with the device caught within patient vasculature, tissue, or other device.

title: Separation failure
definition: Problem associated with the device or one of its components failing to detach or separate as intended.

title: Difficult or delayed separation
definition: Problem associated with users experiencing difficulty or delay with detachment or separation of the device.

title: Premature separation of device
definition: Problem associated with an early and unexpected detachment or separation of the device from the system.

title: Alarm not visible
definition: The device does not display an alarm message when required.

title: No audible alarm
definition: The device fails to emit an audible alarm.

title: Low audible alarm
definition: The audible device alarm cannot be heard clearly.

title: Delayed alarm
definition: The device alarm system operates with delay.

title: False alarm
definition: Problem associated with the device providing incorrect alarm warning or alert to user.

title: Defective alarm
definition: The device alarm does not operate as expected and/or in agreement with device's specifications.

title: Fail-safe did not activate
definition: Problem associated with the device fail-safe mechanism, which did not function or function in a non effective way, compromising safe use of the device.

title: No fail-safe mechanism
definition: The device does not have a fail-safe mechanism, although such mechanism would be required for its appropriate and/or safe functioning.

title: Failure of device to self-test
definition: Problem associated with the device failing to perform an internal self-diagnostic process to ensure normal operation during or prior to use.

title: Failure to auto stop
definition: Problem associated with the inability of device to turn itself off when the device is not in an operable condition.

title: Failure to reset
definition: Problem associated with the device failing to set a variable, register, or other storage location back to a prescribed state.

title: Failure to zero
definition: Problem associated with the device failing to set a variable, register, or other storage location back to zero.

title: Inappropriate or unexpected reset
definition: Problem associated with the device setting a variable, register, or other storage location to an inappropriate or unexpected state.

title: Premature elective replacement indicator
definition: Problems with the early or unexpected activation of the elective replacement indicator.

title: Premature end-of-life indicator
definition: Problem with the early or unexpected activation of the end-of-life indicator.

title: Shielding failure
definition: Problem associated with the device inability to act as a barrier for absorption of radiation energy in X-rays, gamma rays, etc.

title: Accessory incompatible
definition: An accessory required for the intended purpose of the device appears incompatible with the device, thus compromising the intended function of the device.

title: Component incompatible
definition: A component required for the proper functioning of the device is not compatible with other components or subassemblies of the device, thus compromising the intended function of the device.

title: Device-device incompatibility
definition: Problem associated with the incompatibility of two or more devices while being operated in the same use environment thereby leading to a dysfunction of more than one device.

title: Measurement system incompatibility
definition: Problem associated with the incompatibility of the measurement systems between and/or within device systems that are inherent to the individual device thereby leading to miscalculated or mismatched measurements from those devices, e.g., international metric system versus U.S. measurement system.

title: Unintended compatibility
definition: Problem associated with the ability of two or more devices which are intended to be incompatible but are able to work or fit together.

title: Biofilm coating in device
definition: Problem associated with the undesired introduction of a biofilm coating into or onto the device.

title: Contamination of device ingredient or reagent
definition: Problem associated with the undesired introduction of impurities either chemical or microbiological in nature, or of foreign matter into or onto the device ingredient or reagent.

title: Device contamination with body fluid
definition: Problem associated with the undesired presence of body fluid in/on the device, which is not part of the documented device specifications and requirements.

title: Device contamination with chemical or other material
definition: Problem associated with contamination of the device with chemical substance or other non biologic material.

title: Microbial contamination of device
definition: Problem associated with undesired microbial contamination of the device.

title: Device contaminated during manufacture or shipping
Failure to clean adequately
definition: Problem associated with the failure of the device or operator to remove any visible soil, foreign material or organism deposits on the external surfaces, crevices, and joints of the device.

title: Failure to disinfect
definition: Failure to properly disinfect the device when reprocessing it.

title: Flushing problem
definition: Failure to properly disinfect the device when reprocessing it.

title: Problem with sterilisation
definition: Device was not sterilized properly during reprocessing.

title: Residue after decontamination
definition: Problem associated with the decontamination process not adequately removing unwanted visible soil, foreign material, or organism deposits.

title: Ambient noise problem
definition: Problem associated with any undesired acoustic energy or vibration that tends to interfere with the operation of the device.

title: Ambient temperature problem
definition: Problem associated with compromised device performance at the ambient temperature or the storage at an inappropriate ambient temperature.

title: Fumes or vapours as environmental compatibility problem
definition: Problem associated with the visibility, odor, or toxicity of an ambient vapor or gas.

title: Fungus in device environment
definition: Problem associated with the visibility of molds, mildews, yeasts, and/or mushrooms in the immediate environment in which the device is being used.

title: Moisture or humidity problem
definition: Problem associated with an unsatisfactory humidity level in the storage or use environment which affects the device performance.

title: Ventilation problem in device environment
definition: Problem associated with the circulation of fresh air in the immediate atmosphere in which the device is being used.

title: Device unsafe to use in environment
definition: Problem associated with environmental condition that results in the unsafe use of the device. (E.g. electromagnetic fields, noise, vibration, microbiological contamination etc.)

title: Environmental particulates
definition: Problem associated with fine solids or liquid particles such as dust, smoke, fume, and/or mist suspended in the immediate atmosphere in which the device is being used.

title: Medical gas supply problem
definition: Problem associated with the facility-supplied medical gases such as medical air, oxygen, nitrous oxide, and nitrogen.

title: Electrical power problem
definition: Problem associated with the quality of the facility-supplied power.

title: Misassembled during installation
definition: Problem associated with the use of the device characterised by incorrect assembly of device components, parts or constituents.

title: Device markings or labelling problem
definition: Problem associated with the written, printed or graphic material accompanying or affixed to the device or any of its packaging. This includes verbal instructions relating to identification, technical description, and usage provided by the device manufacturers. Problems can include but are not limited to this material being unclear, missing, worn out, incorrect or inaccurate.

title: Lack of maintenance documentation or guidelines
definition: Problem associated with user facility not receiving adequate service documentation, guidelines, or recommendations to perform preventative and corrective maintenance and performance assurance checks.

title: Inadequate instructions for healthcare professional
definition: Problem associated with inaccuracies in any written, printed, or graphic matter that is affixed to the device or its packaging with any matter that accompanies the device including verbal instructions related to identification, technical description and use of device provided by the device manufacturers that is intended for healthcare professionals.

title: Inadequate instructions for non-healthcare professional
definition: Problem associated with users being unclear and not able to follow any written, printed, or graphic matter that is affixed to device or its packaging with any matter that accompanies the device including verbal instructions related to identification, technical description and use of the device provided by the device manufactures that vary from the standard of medical care in a given environment.

title: Inadequate or insufficient training
definition: Problem associated with facility not providing satisfactory initial and/or periodic user training covering operation of the device.

title: Device difficult to set up or prepare
definition: Problem associated with the use of the device in terms of user experiencing difficulty in preparing device for use, even if the operation is being performed according to labeled instructions for use.

title: Device difficult to program or calibrate
definition: The device is difficult to program, calibrate or set to desired state, even by appropriately trained user/operator.

title: Device difficult to maintain
definition: Problem associated with the user's ability to service the device according to the manufacturer specifications relating to the device routine maintenance, i.e., periodic inspection, failure detection, repair, and care of the device to sustain or restore acceptable operating conditions.

title: Inadequate user interface
definition: Problem associated with the means by which the operator and the equipment communicate or interact.

title: Device handling problem
definition: Handling of the device not in accordance with specification, prior to use on the patient.

title: Use of incorrect control settings
definition: Problem associated with the use of the device in terms of inappropriate and false control setting for the device's specified operation and/or intended use.

title: Improper or incorrect procedure or method
definition: Problem associated with the use of the device in terms of nonconforming to that device's intended use, specifications, procedure and process or service instructions and information provided by the device manufacturers.

title: Off-label use
definition: Problem associated with the device which has been used for an unapproved indication or for an unapproved intended use.

title: Misassembled
definition: Problem associated with incorrect assembly of the device or constituents after being put into use.

title: Adverse event without identified device or use problem
definition: An adverse event (e.g. patient harm) appears to have occurred, but there does not appear to have been a problem with the device or the way it was used.

title: No apparent adverse event
definition:
A report has been received but the description provided does not appear to relate to an adverse event.
This code allows a report to be recorded for administration purposes, even if it doesn't meet the requirements for adverse event reporting.

title: Appropriate term or code not available for aspects of incidents related to devices
definition:
The device problem is not adequately described by any other term. 
Note: this code must not be used unless there is no other feasible code. The preferred term should be documented when submitting an adverse event report. This information will be used to determine if a new term should be added to the code table.

title: Cause traced to transport or storage
Cause cannot be traced to device
Cause of problem with device not established
Conclusion not yet available regarding problem with device
Biocompatibility problem with device identified
definition: The device causes cellular or tissue responses that elicit an undesirable local or systemic effect in the recipient or beneficiary of that therapy. (See ISO 10993)

title: Biological contamination of device
definition: The undesirable presence of living organisms such as bacteria, fungi, or viruses or their products (enzymes or toxins).

title: Material or material leachate pyrogenic problem with device identified
definition: The undesirable presence of pyrogens or fever-producing organisms caused by materials that permeate through the device.

title: Cytotoxicity problem with device identified
definition: The device was found to have an undesirable level of toxicity to living cells.

title: Genotoxicity problem with device identified
definition: The device's ability to cause damage to genetic material (e.g. leading to malignant tumors). (See ISO 10993)

title: Hematological problem with device identified
definition: The device affects or impacts the blood or its components. (See ISO 10993 all parts)

title: Unintended presence of allergens in device identified
definition: Unintended or unexpected presence of allergens in the device.
exclusions: Presence of allergen expected but not adequately labelled

title: Reproductive toxicity problem with device identified
definition: The device affects reproductive function, embryo development (teratogenicity), and prenatal and early postnatal development. (ISO 10993 part 3)

title: Electrical or electronic component problem with device identified
definition: The performance of an electrical or electronic component was found to be inadequate.

title: Hardware timing problem with device identified
definition: Problem that results from improper sequential activation of components.

title: Impedance problem with device identified
definition: Problems due to insufficient or excessive resistance to current flow either by the device or circuit.

title: Insulation problem with device identified
definition: Problems due to inadequate or incorrect electrical insulation material.

title: Open circuit in device
definition: Problem due to an electrical circuit that does not conduct current because a switch is open, a wire is broken, etc.

title: Current leakage problem of device identified
definition: Problems related to leakage currents which may cause electric shock. These currents usually flow through the protective ground conductor. In its absence, these currents could flow from the device to the ground via the human body.

title: Energy storage system problem in device
definition: Problems related to the energy storage system (e.g. the rechargeable battery, charging system, or capacitor) and includes problems such as premature power source depletion and battery explosions.

title: Loss of power to device
definition: A device that experienced problems due to a loss in the power supply.

title: Power fluctuation in device
definition: The device failed due to fluctuations within the power supply (e.g. transient power, power spike, power dip, or power sequencing).

title: Short circuit of device
definition: Problems due to an unintentionally low-resistance connection between two points in an electric circuit, resulting in either excessive current flow that often causes damage or in a new shorter circuit that draws current away from the original pathways and components.

title: Signal loss of device
definition: Problems due to the loss or weakening of an electrical signal or signals.

title: Conducted interference with device
definition: Problems related to electromagnetic interference (EMI) by physical contact with conductors (e.g. wires, resistors, terminals) as opposed to radiated EMI which is caused by induction (without physical contact of the conductors).

title: Electrostatic discharge of device
definition: Problems due to sudden and momentary bursts of electrical current flowing between two objects at different electrical potentials.

title: Inadequate immunity of device
definition: Problems related to immunity or capabilities to resist electromagnetic interference (EMI).

title: Unintended emission of device
definition: Problems due to unintended emission of electromagnetic energy by the device.

title: Radiofrequency interference with device identified
definition: Problems due to radiofrequency interference. RFI is a disturbance that affects an electrical circuit due to either electromagnetic conduction or electromagnetic radiation emitted from an external source.

title: Wired communication problem with device identified
definition: Communications problems between devices within a wired system.

title: Wireless communication problem with device identified
definition: Communications problems between devices within a wireless system.

title: Network communication problem with device identified
definition: Communications problems between devices within a network system.

title: Incompatible component or accessory of device identified
definition: A device that malfunctions due to a component(s)/accessory that does not operate correctly and according to the device's specifications.

title: Inadequate labelling or instructions for use of device identified
definition: Inadequate information on the labels or in the instructions for use e.g. steps that are difficult to follow or that are missing.

title: Incorrect labelling or instructions for use of device identified
definition: Missing, incorrect, or inappropriate information on the labels e.g. mislabeled contents or device labeling characteristics or package contents.

title: Inadequate or incorrect instructions for maintenance of device identified
definition: Inadequate or incorrect information in the instructions for maintenance.

title: Degradation problem identified with device
definition: Problems that occur when the device becomes worn, weakened, corroded, or broken down due to processes such as aging, permeation, and corrosion.

title: Inappropriate material identified in device
definition: Problems that occur due to the presence of a material that should not be present or part of the device.

title: Inadequate physicochemical properties identified in device
definition: Problems that occur due to the physicochemical properties.

title: Incompatible material identified in device
definition: Problems that occur due to the incompatibility of materials that co-exist simultaneously as part of the device.

title: Reactivity problem identified with device
definition: Problems that occur due to the reactivity of materials (e.g. over-react or under-react).

title: Tolerance stack-up identified in device
definition: Problems that result from a combination of specification variances of the components.

title: Device migration identified
definition: A device that has moved from its original location due to external forces (e.g. stent or lead movement).

title: Friction problem identified with device
definition: Problems caused by its surface coming in contact with another surface or fluid.

title: Leakage or seal problem identified with device
definition: Problems caused by inadequate/broken seal within the device.

title: Lubrication problem identified with device
definition: Problems that occurred because of the presence of either too much or too little lubricant where required (e.g. connectors, leading to failure mechanisms such as corrosion).

title: Stiffness problem identified with device
definition: Problems caused by either excessive or inadequate physical force exerted on it by another object resulting in problems e.g. wear, bending, deformation, fracture, fatigue.

title: Deformation problem with device identified
definition: Problems caused by changes in the shape or size of the device due to an applied force. This can be a result of tensile forces, compressive forces, shear, bending, tensile (pulling), or torsion.

title: Fatigue problem with device identified
definition: Problems due to the weakening or breakdown of its material when subjected to stress or a series of repeated stresses.

title: Fracture problem with device identified
definition: Problems caused by the separation of a component, object, or material into two or more pieces including shear.

title: Mechanical shock problem with device identified
definition: Problems caused by the sudden violent blow or collision to the whole device (e.g. by dropping).

title: Vibration problem with device identified
definition: Problems caused by the constant rhythmic motion of the device, or something in the environment to which the device is exposed.

title: Wear problem with device identified
definition: Problems due to the premature or expected erosion of its material by use, deterioration, or change.

title: Incorrect dimension of device identified
definition: Problems caused by incorrect physical dimensions of the device or one of its parts

title: Optical transmission problem identified with device
definition: Problems with the device's ability to pass light energy.

title: Light source problem identified with device
definition: Problems with the optical properties of a device such as diopter, glare, and irradiance or glistening.

title: Gradient induced field problem with device identified
definition: Problems that result from the gradient-induced fields generated during radiologic procedures e.g. magnetic resonance imaging.

title: Image artifact identified in device
definition: The unacceptable distortion of an image due to signal loss that may occur during a radiologic procedure such as magnetic resonance imaging.

title: Magnetically-induced movement of device identified
definition: Problems due to unintended or excessive movement created by the application of magnetic fields.

title: Radiofrequency induced overheating of device identified
definition: Problems due to unintended radiofrequency-induced temperature increase that can occur in the vicinity of the device.

title: Configuration issue of device identified
definition: Problems due to change control or incorrect version, including regional requirements.

title: Data compression error in device identified
definition: Data was lost or corrupted during the operation of reducing storage space or communication bandwidth.

title: Incorrect algorithm in device identified
definition: The device software was found to implement an incorrect sequence of steps for a specific computation.

title: Incorrect data definition in device identified
definition: The device software was found to contain errors in specifying or manipulating data items.

title: Interface design error of device identified
definition: The device software was found to contain errors in the user interface (including usability problems) or the interfaces with other systems.

title: Non-functional defect in device identified
definition: The device software contained software errors that did not impact its operation.

title: Software timing problem in device identified
definition: Problem that results from the incorrect sequencing or activation of software modules.

title: Software maintenance problem identified with device
definition: The device software was not maintained/updated properly.

title: Software installation problem identified with device
definition: The device software was not installed as per the specifications or failed to properly install.

title: Software requirement error with device identified
definition: The software requirements for the device are either incomplete, inadequate, or in conflict.

title: Software runtime error in device identified
definition: The device software failed during operation as a result of a coding error.

title: Software security vulnerability of device identified
definition: The device software failed to provide adequate authorization, access control, protection and accountability features.

title: Erroneous data transfer in device identified
definition: The device software fails to transfer the expected data within a system or to another device.

title: Data storage or loss of data problem in device identified
definition: Storage of data was unsuccessful in total or in part.

title: Overheating of device problem identified
definition:
The device was found to become hotter than expected during operation. This applies to devices which are not intended to deliver heat. 
Use "Excessive heating identified" for devices which are intended to deliver heat during operation.
exclusions: Overheating of devices intended to deliver heat during operation

title: Excessive heating of device problem identified
definition:
The device delivered more heat than intended or expected during operation.
This applies to devices which are intended to deliver heat.
Use "Overheating problem identified" for devices which are not intended to deliver heat during operation.
exclusions: Overheating of device not intended to deliver heat during operation

title: Inadequate cooling of device problem identified
definition: The device did not sufficiently cool the patient or another device during operation.

title: Fail-safe problem with device identified
definition: A system intended to prevent unsafe operation of the device did not operate correctly.

title: Alarm system problem with device identified
definition: A system intended to warn of a potentially unsafe condition did not operate correctly.

title: Problem of device to self-test identified
definition: Malfunction of the device's self-test system.

title: Problem of device to auto stop identified
definition: An auto stop function of a device did not operate correctly.

title: Reset problem with device identified
definition: The device does not reset properly.

title: Premature indicator activation problem identified
definition: A system intended to indicate the device status was triggered prematurely.

title: Shielding problem with device identified
definition: Inadequate shielding of/by the device.

title: Missing or inadequate safety measures of device identified
definition: Safety measures are inadequately applied or missing.

title: Device incorrectly cleaned during reprocessing identified
definition: The cleaning procedure is not followed correctly or used inappropriate cleaning materials.

title: Device incorrectly disinfected or sterilised during reprocessing identified
definition: The disinfection or sterilization process was incorrect or the wrong products for disinfection or sterilization were used.

title: Device incorrectly assembled during reprocessing identified
definition: Incorrect assembly of the device following reprocessing.

title: Failure to calibrate problem identified
definition: A device that cannot calibrate (establish the relationship between a measuring device and the units of measure) to ensure accurate readings.

title: Device difficult to operate problem identified
definition: Problems including set-up, operation, and disassembly of equipment. Not including reprocessing.

title: Incorrect interpretation of results or data problem identified
definition: Problems resulting from the incorrect interpretation by the user of the results or data provided by the device.

title: New or unknown interferent problem with device identified
definition: New or unknown endogenous or exogenous interferent (sample) identified.

title: Known interferent problem with device identified
definition: Known interferent in the sample identified.

title: Pre-analytical handling problem with device identified
definition: Incorrect pre-analytical handling of patient's sample by the user.

title: Environmental temperature problem with device identified
definition: Device performance was affected by the temperature, or changes in temperature, of the environment in which it was used.

title: Dust or dirt problem with device identified
definition: A device that experienced problems due to ingress, or coating, of dust or dirt.

title: Contamination of environment by device identified
definition: Operation of the device results in contamination of the nearby environment e.g. dust, dirt, smoke, heat or biological material.

title: Environmental pressure problem with device identified
definition: Device performance was affected by the pressure, or changes in pressure, of the environment in which it was used.

title: Ambient light problem with device identified
definition:
Device performance was affected by ambient light.
This term applies to the direct effects of ambient light on the device, and to the user's ability to operate the device (e.g. to read device output).

title: Environmental humidity problem with device identified
definition: Device performance was affected by the humidity, or changes in humidity, of the environment in which it was used.

title: Assembly problem with device identified
definition: Problems that occurred because the device was assembled incorrectly.

title: Sterilization problem with device identified
definition: Problems that occurred during terminal sterilization by the manufacturer.

title: Installation problem with device identified
definition: A device that malfunctions because it was incorrectly installed, set up, or configured (E.g. misconfiguration of an "automatic" defibrillator to "semi-automatic", thereby leading to failure).

title: Maintenance of manufacturing machinery problem with device identified
definition: Problems caused by failure to maintain manufacturing equipment used to produce the device.

title: Packaging of device compromised problem identified
definition: Problems that occurred because of a compromised packaging of the device (e.g. broken or incomplete seal).

title: Packaging materials of device problem identified
definition: Problems that occurred because the composition or type of packaging materials was inappropriate for the device.

title: Packaging of device contains unintended material problem identified
definition: Problems that occurred because unintended material was packaged with the device.

title: Packaging contains incorrect device problem identified
definition: Problems that occurred because the packaging contained an incorrect device.

title: Maintenance problem with device identified
definition: A device malfunction or problem that occurs after production because the device was not properly maintained according to the instructions (e.g. maintenance may be performed by user facility, distributor, or service provider).

title: Storage of device problem identified
definition: Problems that result from storing the device in an uncontrolled or improper environment (e.g. moisture sensitive devices stored in a humid environment).

title: No device problem found
definition: The device either functioned as intended or a problem was not found.

title: No findings available
definition: Use when no investigation can be performed and therefore no results will be obtained.

title: Results pending completion of investigation
definition: Investigation is ongoing and results are not yet available. Do not use this code if the investigation is complete.

title: Appropriate term or code for investigation of device not available
definition:
Problems not adequately described by any other term. 
Note: This code must not be used unless there is no other feasible code. The preferred term should be documented when submitting an adverse event report. This information will be used to determine if a new term should be added to the code table.

title: Testing of actual or suspected device
definition: The investigation employed relevant empirical testing of the actual device suspected in the reported adverse event in order to establish their functional and other properties and to identify possible causes for the adverse event. Relevant testing would typically be based on test methods used for evaluating safety and performance as described in the latest relevant standards.

title: Testing of device from same lot or batch retained by manufacturer
definition: The investigation employed relevant empirical testing of the device of the same lot or batch as that of the suspected device in the reported adverse event in order to support the identification of possible causes for the adverse event. Testing was performed using the device retained by the manufacturer (i.e. was not shipped). Relevant testing would typically be based on test methods used for evaluating safety and performance as described in the latest relevant standards.

title: Testing of device from same lot batch returned from user
definition: The investigation employed relevant empirical testing of the device of the same lot or batch as that of the suspected device in the reported adverse event in order to support the identification of possible causes for the adverse event. The device was returned from the user. Relevant testing would typically be based on test methods used for evaluating safety and performance as described in the latest relevant standards.

title: Testing of device from other lot batch retained by manufacturer
definition: The investigation employed relevant empirical testing of the device of another lot or batch than that of the suspected device in the reported adverse event in order to support the identification of possible causes for the adverse event. This includes devices without a lot/batch designation. Testing was performed using the device retained by the manufacturer (i.e. was not shipped). Relevant testing would typically be based on test methods used for evaluating safety and performance as described in the latest relevant standards.

title: Testing of device from other lot or batch returned from user
definition: The investigation employed relevant empirical testing of the device of another lot or batch than that of the suspected device in the reported adverse event in order to support the identification of possible causes for the adverse event. This includes devices without a lot/batch designation. The device was returned from the user. Relevant testing would typically be based on test methods used for evaluating safety and performance as described in the technical file. Relevant testing would typically be based on test methods used for evaluating safety and performance as described in the latest relevant standards.

title: Testing of model variant
definition: The investigation employed relevant empirical testing of a model variant of the device involved in the reported adverse event in order to support the identification of possible causes for the adverse event through plausibility reasoning. A model variant is not identical to the actual device, but shares relevant characteristics with the device involved. Relevant testing would typically be based on test methods used for evaluating safety and performance as described in the latest relevant standards.

title: Testing of raw or starting materials
definition: The investigation employed relevant empirical testing of the materials used in construction of the device involved in the reported adverse event in order to support the identification of possible causes for the adverse event. Relevant testing would typically be based on test methods used for evaluating safety and performance as described in the latest relevant standards.

title: Testing of patient sample or reference material using manufacturer's device
definition: The investigation employed relevant empirical testing of a patient sample or reference material using the device (usually an IVD) involved in the reported adverse event in order to support the identification of possible causes for the adverse event. Relevant testing would typically be based on test methods used for evaluating safety and performance as described in the latest relevant standards.

title: Testing of patient sample or reference material using reference method
definition: The investigation employed relevant empirical testing of a patient sample or reference material using an appropriate reference method to the device (usually an IVD) involved in the reported adverse event in order to support the identification of possible causes for the adverse event. Relevant testing would typically be based on test methods used for evaluating safety and performance as described in the latest relevant standards.

title: Testing of patient sample or reference material using competitor's device
definition: The investigation employed relevant empirical testing of a patient sample or reference material using a competitor's device that is comparable to the device (usually an IVD) involved in the reported adverse event in order to support the identification of possible causes for the adverse event. Relevant testing would typically be based on test methods used for evaluating safety and performance as described in the latest relevant standards.

title: Historical data analysis
definition: The investigation involved the analysis of historical adverse events data of the actual device involved in the adverse event and/or of products from the same and/or different batches/lots.

title: Trend analysis
definition: The investigation involved trend analysis of adverse event of the actual device involved in the adverse event and/or of products from the same and/or different batches/lots. It should be noted that trend analysis typically is not considered sufficient as a stand-alone method, but should be used in conjunction with other investigation methods for providing for instance complementary information.

title: Communication or interviews
definition: The investigation involved communication/interviews (either interpersonal or through technical means, e.g. phone, e-mail) with persons close to the adverse event, e.g. healthcare professionals (doctors, nurses etc.), the affected patient(s) or other users including, where appropriate, relatives or others engaged in caring for the affected patient.

title: Analysis of production records
definition: The investigation involved the analysis of relevant production records in view of supporting the identification of possible causes for the adverse event.

title: Analysis of data provided by user or third party
definition: The investigation involved the analysis of relevant data provided by the user (e.g. healthcare professional, patient, clinical engineer) or a third party (e.g. testing facility) in view of supporting the identification of possible causes for the adverse event.

title: Device not manufactured by reporting manufacturer
definition: Further information was obtained which established that the manufacturer of the device involved was not the one to which it was initially attributed.

title: Device not returned
definition: The actual device involved in the adverse event was not returned for testing despite requests by manufacturer.

title: Device discarded
definition: The actual device involved in the adverse event had been already discarded and thus irretrievably lost for testing.

title: Incomplete device returned
definition: The device was returned incompletely, lacking parts, components or accessories that would be required for appropriate testing and analysis of root causes.

title: Device not accessible for testing
definition: The actual device involved in the adverse event is not readily accessible for testing (e.g. remains implanted in patient).

title: Type of investigation not yet determined
definition: Details to determine the type of investigation are not yet available, but are being sought. Do not use this code if the investigation is complete.

title: Insufficient information available
definition: The information available relating to the reported event is not sufficient to identify either the manufacturer, the device, or other essential information. This term indicates that no further investigation is possible. Do not use this code if further information is being sought, instead use ''Type of investigation not yet determined''.

title: Absorber component of medical device
definition: A component or material designed to take in or attenuate a substance.

title: Cautery tip component of medical device
definition: A component designed to coagulate and seal blood vessels or to destroy tissue with heat or electric current.

title: Device ingredient or reagent component of medical device
definition: A consumable material that is added to a device and is used to make a finished product or becomes part of a finished product.

title: Gas scavenging component of medical device
definition: A component designed to remove certain gases from a gas stream or environment.

title: Monomer liquid component of medical device
definition: The liquid that reacts with the polymer powder to form adhesive, resin, or cement.

title: Test strip component of medical device
definition: A piece of chemically treated medium designed to react in the presence of specific amounts of a trigger substance.

title: Polymer powder component of medical device
definition: The powder that reacts with the monomer liquid to form adhesive, resin, or cement.

title: Antenna component of medical device
definition: A component designed to transmit or receive electromagnetic signals.

title: Battery component of medical device
definition: A component designed to produce an electric current through chemical reaction.

title: Battery charger component of medical device
definition: A device designed to restore the capacity of a battery.

title: Cable grip component of medical device
definition: Component used for tensioning, pulling or stringing of wires and cables.

title: Cable sleeve component of medical device
definition: Component used to protect cables and wires from abrasion, moisture and the elements.

title: Circuit board component of medical device
definition: A non-conducting board with conductive tracks and electronic components forming a circuit.

title: Circuit breaker component of medical device
definition: A component designed to open an electrical circuit when it becomes overloaded.

title: Computer processor component of medical device
definition: Component that carries out the instructions of a computer program by performing the basic arithmetic, logic, controlling, and input/output operations specified by the instructions.

title: Memory or storage component of medical device
definition: Any component that can hold data in machine-readable format.

title: Network interface component of medical device
definition: Point of interconnection between a computer and other computer that are linked each other.

title: Computer software driver component of medical device
definition: A computer interface designed to control the interaction between a CPU and a peripheral device.

title: Software interface component of medical device
definition: Languages, codes and messages that programs use to communicate with each other and to the hardware.

title: User interface component of medical device
definition: A computer program that controls the interaction between a user and a system.

title: Cooling module component of medical device
definition: A component designed to lower the temperature of a device or system.

title: Device programmer component of medical device
definition: A piece of hardware for transferring data onto programmable integrated circuits.

title: Device reader component of medical device
definition: A piece of hardware used to read the memory and the properties of a device.

title: Electrical capacitor component of medical device
definition: An electrical component designed to store an electric charge.

title: Electrical fuse component of medical device
definition: An electrical component designed to stop the flow of current when an overload condition exists.

title: Electrical inductor component of medical device
definition: A component designed to introduce electromotive force to a circuit, usually a coil surrounding a wire.

title: Electrical resistor component of medical device
definition: An electronic component that opposes the flow of current.

title: Electrical solenoid component of medical device
definition: An electronic component consisting of a coil surrounding a movable iron core that is designed to act as a switch or relay.

title: Electrical transducer component of medical device
definition: An electrical component that converts one form of energy into another.

title: Electrical semiconductor component of medical device
definition: A type of electronic component, including transistors and diodes, that make use of the variable conductivity of certain materials.

title: Integrated circuit chip component of medical device
definition: A microelectronic circuit that incorporates many interconnected transistors and other components.

title: Display indicator component of medical device
definition: A component designed to show an operating condition of a system or to attract attention.

title: Display screen component of medical device
definition: A panel or area on an electronic device where images and data are displayed.

title: Electrode component of medical device
definition: A small piece of metal or other conductive substance that is used to take an electric current to or from a source of power, a piece of equipment. Not to be used for patient connection.

title: Ground strap or wire component of medical device
definition: A wire cable or strap designed to carry current safely away from an electronic device under fault condition

title: Wiring harness component of medical device
definition: A collection of grouped wires or cables designed to connect to a specific device.

title: Electrical mixer component of medical device
definition: An electronic component designed to blend signals.

title: Electrical port component of medical device
definition: An electronic circuit that acts as a connection to another device or component.

title: Emitter component of medical device
definition: The electron source electrode in a transistor or any source in a system.

title: Headphone or headset component of medical device
definition: A component that covers the ear through which you can listen without other people hearing. Some headphones come with a microphone which is used to communicate with other people or the device.

title: Heater component of medical device
definition: A piece of equipment used to raise the temperature of air, gas or water or an object.

title: Hub component of medical device
definition: An electronic component designed as a central connection for other devices or components.

title: Inverter component of medical device
definition: An electrical component that converts direct current to alternating current.

title: Magnet component of medical device
definition: A component that attracts iron and produces a magnetic field.

title: Oscillator component of medical device
definition: An electronic component designed to produce a wave signal.

title: Lead conductor component of medical device
definition: A cable designed to conduct electricity from the device to the lead.

title: Patient electrode component of medical device
definition: An electrical conductor that is designed to make contact with a patient including defibrillator paddles.

title: Power cord component of medical device
definition: A flexible cable designed to connect an electrical device to a power outlet.

title: Power supply component of medical device
definition: Component designed to supply electrical power to devices.

title: Pressure transducer probe component of medical device
definition: A probe component designed to convert a change in pressure into a varying electrical signal.

title: Printer component of medical device
definition: An electronic component that is designed to transfer text or images to paper or other substrate.

title: Receiver component of medical device
definition: An electronic component designed to capture an incoming electromagnetic signal and convert it to an audible or visual signal.

title: Receiver stimulator unit component of medical device
definition: As part of a cochlear implant, an implanted component designed to receive signal from the external component and then decode the signal and transmit it to the brain.

title: Scanner component of medical device
definition: An electronic component that generates a digital representation of an image for data input to a computer~ or a receiver designed to search for a signal within a specified frequency range.

title: Speaker or sounder component of medical device
definition: A component designed to convert electrical signals to sounds that can be heard.

title: Power switch component of medical device
definition: A switch designed to regulate the power to a device.

title: Relay component of medical device
definition: A electronic component designed to break or change the connections in a circuit.

title: Telemetry component of medical device
definition: Component designed to transmit and receive data from a remote source using telecommunications methods.

title: Temperature compensator component of medical device
definition: A component designed to compensate the temperature of one system in response to temperature changes in another system or the environment.

title: Thermostat component of medical device
definition: A component designed to regulate temperature by controlling the starting and stopping of a heating/cooling system.

title: Transformer component of medical device
definition: A component that either steps up or steps down an alternating electrical current to an output that is suitable for another electrical device or component.

title: Transmitter component of medical device
definition: A component to propagate electromagnetic waves.

title: Joystick component of medical device
definition: A control component that uses a movable handle to create two-axis input to a computer.

title: Keyboard or keypad component of medical device
definition: A component consisting of mechanical keys that are pressed to create input to a computer.

title: Microphone component of medical device
definition: A component designed to convert sound to an electrical signal.

title: Touchscreen component of medical device

title: Oxygen analyzer component of medical device

title: Aperture component of medical device

title: Calibrator component of medical device
definition: A standard or reference material used to set the operating parameters of an instrument.

title: Clock component of medical device
definition: A component designed to indicate the time of day.

title: Counter component of medical device
definition: A component designed to keep track of the number of times something happens.

title: Curvette component of medical device

title: Flowmeter component of medical device
definition: A component designed to measure the flow rate of a fluid.

title: Manometer component of medical device
definition: A component designed to measure pressure.

title: Thermometer component of medical device
definition: A component designed to measure temperature.

title: Marker component of medical device
definition: A visual indicator of position, place or route, including radiopaque markers.

title: Pipette component of medical device
definition: A measuring component, traditionally including a graduated tube, designed for the accurate transfer of liquid volumes.

title: Pointer component of medical device
definition: An indicator component designed to show a position on a scale.

title: Scale component of medical device
definition: A component designed for weighing or an indicator component with a graduated sequence of divisions.

title: Bubble sensor component of medical device

title: Oxygen sensor component of medical device
definition: A sensor designed to respond to the presence or level of oxygen in a space or environment.

title: Photodetector component of medical device

title: Pressure sensor component of medical device
definition: A sensor designed to respond to the level of pressure in a space or pressing on a surface.

title: Sensor probe component of medical device
definition: A component designed to reach into a location for sensing. Should be used when the problem involves the probe, not the sensor.

title: Temperature sensor component of medical device
definition: A sensor designed to respond to the temperature of a space, surface, or environment.

title: Timer component of medical device
definition: A component designed to measure a time interval.

title: Access port component of medical device
definition: A component designed for the introduction or removal of medical equipment or any substance.

title: Actuator component of medical device
definition: A component which moves or controls a mechanism or system.

title: Adaptor component of medical device
definition: A component designed to make different pieces of apparatus compatible.

title: Air eliminator component of medical device
definition: A component designed to remove air from a space or fluid.

title: Anchor component of medical device
definition: A component designed to fix the device or a portion of the device in place.

title: Applicator component of medical device
definition: A component designed to transfer a substance to a surface.

title: Automatic injection system component of medical device
definition: A system designed to pump a gas or liquid into a space or environment without outside intervention.

title: Bag component of medical device
definition: A flexible container, often with a single opening.

title: Ball component of medical device
definition: Any component that is spherical in shape.

title: Balloon component of medical device
definition: A sac designed to be inflated and deflated.

title: Bearings component of medical device
definition: A point of contact between moving parts designed for support or to reduce friction.

title: Potting component of medical device
definition: The resin, glue or other substance used to seal other components together.

title: Probe component of medical device
definition: A component designed to reach into a location for manipulating or for measuring.

title: Processor component of medical device

exclusions: Computer processor component of medical device

title: Pulley component of medical device
definition: A component which changes the direction of a force (e.g. belt or chain).

title: Pump component of medical device
definition: A component designed to facilitate the movement of a fluid.

title: Pusher component of medical device
definition: A component designed to advance something by pushing it.

title: Rachet component of medical device
definition: A part that allows or forces another part's movement in a single direction.

title: Side rail component of medical device
definition: A supportive or protective rail attached to the side of something.

title: Regulator component of medical device
definition: A component designed to control a process or condition.

title: Reservoir component of medical device
definition: A vessel designed to store a fluid.

title: Bellows component of medical device
definition: A component that expands and contracts to draw in air through a valve or orifice and expels it through a tube.

title: Ring component of medical device
definition: A circular band-shaped component.

title: Rod or shaft component of medical device

title: Seal component of medical device

title: Shock absorber component of medical device
definition: A mechanical component designed to dampen or attenuate a force.

title: Sleeve component of medical device
definition: A cylindrical fitting that slides over another part of a device or other object.

title: Slide component of medical device
definition: A flat rectangular piece of glass on which specimens can be mounted for microscopic study.

title: Socket component of medical device
definition: A component designed as an opening into which something else fits.

title: Spacer component of medical device
definition: A component designed to position objects further apart.

title: Spring component of medical device
definition: An elastic component designed to bend under a load and then return to its shape when unloaded.

title: Stand component of medical device
definition: A support component designed to hold an object.

title: Belt component of medical device
definition: A component consisting of a narrow band of material moving over shafts or pulleys.

title: Steering wire component of medical device
definition: A wire designed to enable a device to be maneuvered.

title: Stent component of medical device
definition: Tubular support placed inside a blood vessel, canal, or duct to aid healing or relieve an obstruction.

title: Stopcock component of medical device
definition: A rotating component designed to act like a tap for regulating the flow of a fluid, and to completely stop the flow when closed fully.

title: Stopper component of medical device
definition: Component designed to close an opening.

title: Strain relief component of medical device
definition: A structure designed to function with a connector to prevent damage to a hose or cable from excess flexing.

title: Stylet component of medical device
definition: A thin metal wire designed to be passed through a needle, catheter, or cannula to stiffen it or clear it of debris.

title: Syringe component of medical device
definition: A component designed as a rigid cylinder with a plunger at one end and a delivery opening at the other.

title: Table component of medical device
definition: A component having a smooth flat surface that is usually supported by one or more vertical legs.

title: Tip component of medical device
definition: Pointed or rounded end of an object.

title: Tool component of medical device
definition: A component that is delivered with a device to support its assembly or operation, and is not part of the device. Use only in the case there are no other terms to describe the component.

title: Bottle component of medical device
definition: A rigid or semi-rigid container used to store liquid.

title: Translational motion component of medical device
definition: A rotating part which is intended to transfer rotational movement or motion to another type of movement (e.g. excenters).

title: Trap component of medical device
definition: A component designed to capture or remove bubbles or fluid.

title: Trocar component of medical device
definition: A sharp, pointed rod designed to pierce the wall of a body cavity to withdraw fluid or allow placement of a catheter or other device.

title: Capillary tube component of medical device
definition: A narrow tube in which a liquid flows up against gravity.

title: Control valve component of medical device
definition: A valve designed to regulate the flow of a fluid or gas.

title: Luer valve component of medical device
definition: A valve that incorporates a Luer fitting.

title: One-way valve component of medical device
definition: A valve designed to allow flow in only one direction.

title: Vaporiser component of medical device

title: Vibrator component of medical device
definition: A mechanical component designed to create a vibratory motion.

title: Washer component of medical device
definition: A flattened disk used as a mechanical seal between objects.

title: Weld component of medical device
definition: Any joining connection that is the result of welding 2 or more parts.

title: Wheel component of medical device
definition: A mechanical component consisting of a spoked, circular rim or solid disk designed to rotate on an axle or shaft.

title: Breathing circuit component of medical device
definition: In an anesthesia machine, the pathway by which a gaseous anesthetic agent and oxygen are delivered to the patient and carbon dioxide is removed. The circuit can be open or closed.

title: Window component of medical device
definition: A transparent panel in a device designed for visual inspection or to let light pass.

title: Shutter component of medical device
definition: Aperture that controls or blocks light or radiation passing through.

title: Brush component of medical device
definition: A component consisting of hairs or bristles set into a handle or holder.

title: Brushing component of medical device
definition: A cylindrical metal sleeve designed to reduce the friction of a rotating shaft.

title: Cable, mechanical structural component of medical device
definition: A long, thin, multistranded rope or metallic wire to hold the subject.

title: Cannula hub component of medical device
definition: A metal or plastic component that connects to the cannula.

title: Cap component of medical device
definition: A component designed to close an opening of a container or device.

title: Carrier component of medical device
definition: A component designed to facilitate the support, movement, or transport of another device or object.

title: Caster component of medical device
definition: A pivoting roller or wheel designed to attach to an object to make it movable.

title: Catheter hub component of medical device
definition: A small metal or plastic component that connects to the catheter.

title: Cell component of medical device
definition: A component that is designed as a container to collect and/or transfer materials, reagents or specimens.

title: Chain component of medical device
definition: Assembly of interconnected links, typically made of metal used for connecting other components.

title: Chamber component of medical device
definition: A component designed as a reservoir/storage.

title: Chassis or frame component of medical device
definition: A supporting frame designed to hold other components or devices such as the internal frame of an electronic device.

title: Clutch component of medical device
definition: A component that engages and disengages power transmission.

title: Coating material component of medical device
definition: A layer of material covering the surface of a device.

title: Helifix coil component of medical device
